view.accounts.tip.base_currency: Three-letter ISO currency code used for all dollar values
view.accounts.tip.create: Persist the account and refresh the account strip in the top bar
view.accounts.th.name: Name
view.cov_denoiser.label.t: T (observations used to estimate the cov)
view.import.dropzone: Drop CSV here, or click to pick.
view.iv_backtest.label.implied: Implied move (%) — what the ATM straddle is pricing for the event
view.api_tokens.label.name: Name
view.earnings_iv.h2.implied_vs_realized_: Implied move vs median realized move — rich/cheap diagonal
view.squeeze_alerts.tts.test_phrase: TraderView squeeze alerts active. AAPL squeezing, up 5.2 percent on 4 times average volume.
view.alert_rules.tts.test_phrase: Custom alert rules active. AAPL squeezing, up 5 percent on triple volume.
view.accounts_overview.h2.winrate_chart: Win rate per account vs 50% baseline
view.accounts_overview.empty_wr_chart: No accounts to chart win rate.
view.accounts_overview.hint.intro: Side-by-side snapshot of every account you own. P/L windows (today / MTD / YTD) are computed from trades.opened_at UTC dates. Open-position fields pull fresh quotes (60s server cache) and aggregate the same way the Live P/L tab does per-account.
view.api_tokens.hint.intro: Personal Access Tokens authenticate third-party integrations against the same endpoints the UI uses. Pass them as Authorization: Bearer pat_<prefix>_<secret>. Tokens are argon2-hashed at rest — the secret is shown once at creation time and never recoverable afterwards. Revoke a token to cut access immediately.
view.api_tokens.warn.save_now: Save this token now — it will never be shown again:
view.backtest_presets.hint.intro: Save parameter combinations under a name; mark them public to get a shareable slug; fork others' public presets to your own library. Run a preset by feeding its preset JSON straight into the Backtest tab.
view.boards.hint.no_news: No cached news for {symbol} — hit Poll now on the News tab to seed.
view.compare.hint.too_few: Only {count} symbol(s) returned data — need at least 2.
view.csv_wizard.hint.intro: Upload any CSV — Notion exports, Excel sheets, hand-rolled logs. The wizard auto-detects headers, you map them to the seven canonical fields below, preview the first 20 rows post-mapping, then commit. Re-imports of the same file are blocked at the imports.sha256 unique constraint; per-row dedupe relies on executions(broker_order_id, executed_at, symbol, side, qty, price).
view.discipline.hint.intro: Streaks computed from chronological closed-trade P/L sign. Rule-violation tracker joins your trade_plans rows to filled trades via linked_trade_id and grades on four checks: stop_set, stop_honored, qty_within (≤ 1.10× planned), direction_match. Discipline % = passing / linked across week / month / all-time.
view.earnings_cal.hint.intro: Polls Yahoo's quoteSummary earnings module every 6h across your watchlist symbols. Surprise % = (actual − estimate) / |estimate| × 100. Reaction columns are next-session and 5-session price moves vs the close on / just before the earnings date, computed from cached daily bars.
view.earnings_cal.hint.empty: No upcoming earnings in the next {days} days. Hit "Poll now" to seed from Yahoo.
view.equity_forecast.hint.intro: Bootstraps R-multiples from your closed-trade history and resamples thousands of forward paths. Each step risks risk_pct × equity and gains equity × risk × R where R is drawn with replacement. Ruin = equity drops to ≤ ruin_threshold of starting (default 50%); ruined paths freeze at zero for the rest of the horizon so percentile bands stay meaningful at the bottom of the fan.
view.fill_quality.hint.intro: Bar-level approximation — TraderView caches daily OHLC, not intraday bid/ask, so this is a directional metric not tick-perfect slippage. fill-in-range places the fill on the day's high-low line; the fill-efficiency column flips that for buys (lower = better) and sells (higher = better) so 100% always means "best possible fill within the day's range". Slippage bps = deviation from typical price (HLC/3), sign-flipped so positive = worse than typical for that side.
view.footprint.hint.ticks: One tick per line: bar_id price volume side where side ∈ {buy, sell, uncertain}. Demo loads 4 bars with engineered patterns: balanced churn → absorption at low → drive up → rejection at high.
view.footprint.hint.grid: Each column = one bar. Each row = one price level. Cells show bid × ask; row color is the per-cell delta (green = ask won, red = bid won). Gold-highlighted row = bar POC (most-traded level).
view.goals.hint.intro: Set monthly / quarterly / yearly P/L + win-rate + max-drawdown targets. Progress is computed live from closed trades whose opened_at falls in the window. Run-rate projection extrapolates current P/L pace to the end of the period so you know if you're on track today.
view.intraday_heatmap.hint.format: One line per trade: timestamp pnl. Timestamps accept ISO 8601 (2024-01-15T14:30:00Z) or bare HH:MM (anchored to a fixed epoch date).
view.journal_ai.hint.defaults: Defaults: Anthropic → https://api.anthropic.com, OpenAI → https://api.openai.com, Ollama → http://localhost:11434. The prompt asks the model to return strict JSON with summary / mistakes / risk_gaps / suggestions / rule_changes arrays.
view.journal.empty.day: No entries for this day yet.
view.liquidity.hint.format: One line per trade: symbol qty net_pnl. Negative pnl = loss. Other Trade fields are auto-filled.
view.market_profile.hint.format: One line per bracket: bracket_index high low. Each bracket prints one letter (A, B, C, …) at every quantized price level it traded through. Demo loads a 13-bracket A-M session shaped like a typical normal-day profile.
view.mood_analytics.hint.intro: Two ingestion paths: per-trade (journal entry directly tied to a trade — cleanest signal) and per-day (daily mood × every trade opened that day). The Pearson correlation is across the union. Positive ρ means happier-mood days produce better outcomes; negative means good moods correlate with overconfidence and losses.
view.new_trade.hint.risk_gate: Pre-trade Risk Gate rules evaluate stop_loss + has_attached_plan before submission. Configure rules in the Risk Gate view.
view.news_event.hint.position_format: One line per position: symbol qty.
view.news_event.hint.event_format: One event per line: event_name <low|medium|high|critical> [comma,sep,symbols]. Omit symbols for market-wide events (e.g., FOMC, NFP). Event-name can contain spaces; the parser finds the impact token automatically.
view.oi_change.hint.format: One line per strike: strike call_oi put_oi call_baseline put_baseline. Baseline = trailing 20-day average (or your own reference). Demo loads an 8-strike chain with engineered surges on the 510 call and 470 put.
view.order_staleness.hint.format: One line per order: id symbol placed_at [last_modified_at] side. Timestamps accept ISO 8601 (2024-06-15T10:00:00Z). Side is one of buy / sell / buy_stop / sell_stop. Modifying an order resets the staleness clock — treated as "re-confirming intent."
view.premarket.hint.none_today: No high-importance scheduled releases today (per static economic calendar). Updated {time}.
view.pyramid.hint.format: One tranche per line: trigger_price qty. Pyramid-Up: tranches must move INTO profit. Scale-In: tranches must move AGAINST you within your planned ladder. Misordered tranches are flagged by both the local pre-flight and the backend.
view.risk_gate.hint.kill_switch: Halt every trade entry across all rules with one click. Toggles a kill_switch rule that always blocks. Disable here when ready to resume.
view.sector_rotation.hint.intro: For each SPDR sector ETF (XLK/XLF/XLE/XLV/XLY/XLP/XLI/XLB/XLU/XLRE/XLC), relative-strength versus SPY across 5/20/60-day windows. Ranks color the cells: rank 1-3 = green (leadership), 4-7 = grey (in line), 8-11 = red (laggards). The sparkline column shows daily sector_return − SPY_return for the last 60 sessions — a rising line means the sector is gaining ground on the index. Refreshes every 5 min.
view.sector_rotation.hint.computed: Computed {time}. Sparkline = (sector daily % return) − (SPY daily % return) over the last 60 sessions.
view.squeeze_alerts.hint.ticks: One tick per line: symbol unix_ts price volume. Demo loads 3 symbols over 30 minutes — AAPL ramps +8% in the last 5 minutes on 4× volume (normal alert), SMID gaps +12% on 6× volume (CRITICAL alarm), MSFT drifts (no alert).
view.stop_loss_best_of.hint.format: One trade per line: entry mae mfe actual_exit. MAE/MFE are POSITIVE excursion magnitudes (max-adverse / max-favorable in dollars). Demo loads 20 mixed-outcome trades with realistic excursion ranges.
view.stress_test.hint.legs: One leg per line: symbol kind spot strike dte iv contracts entry_price. Multiplier is fixed at 100 (equity options). Demo loads a short SPY iron condor.
view.tax_lots.hint.intro: Lot-by-lot accounting derived from your executions. Methods supported: FIFO (default — IRS default for equities), LIFO. Wash-sale flag: per IRC §1091, any loss realized within ±30 days of a buy of the same symbol is disallowed for tax purposes (the disallowed amount is added back to net gain). Long-term = held ≥ 365 days. Short/cover events are passed through but not lot-matched in v1.
view.trade_compare.hint.intro: Pick 2-4 closed trades from the picker. The right pane shows side-by-side stats and a normalized P/L overlay where the x-axis is t ∈ [0..1] from open to close (so a 5-min scalp and a 6-month swing can be compared on the same chart) and the y-axis is % return vs entry. Auto-picks bar interval per trade duration (1m / 5m / 1h / 1d) so each curve has 50-200 points.
view.tutorial.risk_gate.intro: Discipline that enforces instead of just reporting. The gate sits between the new-trade form and the broker — every submission runs the rules first.
view.vol_surface.hint.intro: IV grid across moneyness × expiration. Color intensity shows IV relative to the surface min/max — bright red = highest IV (richest options), deep blue = lowest (cheapest). Use OTM puts (negative moneyness) and OTM calls (positive) to read skew. Watch for term-structure inversion (front-month IV > back-month): usually signals an upcoming binary event.
view.vol_surface.hint.fetching: Fetching {sym} chain across {n} expirations (this calls Yahoo once per expiration)…
view.vol_surface.range: Surface range: {min}% → {max}% (IV expressed as annualized standard deviation, decimal × 100).
view.vpin.hint.toxic: VPIN ≥ {threshold} marks a "toxic" bucket — informed traders running through the book. Was used to flag the 2010 flash crash minutes ahead of the cascade.
view.vpin.hint.chart: Red dashed = {threshold} toxic threshold. Spikes above the line are flow-toxicity alerts.
view.vwap_slippage.hint.format: One line per bar: typical_price volume. Typical = (high+low+close)/3. Pre-computed by the caller so this view stays agnostic about whether you're feeding 1-second, 1-minute, or 1-hour bars. Demo loads 200 bars with an intentional below-VWAP long fill.
view.webhooks.hint.intro: Fan-out alerts to Discord, Slack, or any generic HTTP endpoint. Wire a webhook here, then reference its ID from an alert rule's webhook_ids[] field — alert fires call POST automatically.

view.news_event.empty.actions: No trim actions — no high-impact events affecting open positions.
view.range_expansion.empty.events: No range-expansion events at current config.
view.squeeze_alerts.empty.events: No squeezes detected at current thresholds.

view.trade_plan_checklist.placeholder.th: Why does this work? Catalyst? Setup? Confirmation?
view.exports.row.realized_desc: Form 8949-style rows: acquired/disposed, basis, proceeds, gain/loss, wash-sale.
view.exports.row.open_lots_desc: Year-end open inventory: per-lot qty, cost-per-share, basis, holding-period.
view.exports.row.tax_package_desc: One-page report: summary cards + realized table + open-lots table + §1091 notes.
view.var_estimator.hint.fat_tail: Positive Hist − Gauss = empirical tail is fatter than Gaussian assumes.
view.tutorial.trading.webull: Read-only. Order entry is intentionally not implemented. Open webull.com → DevTools → Network → copy did, access_token, t_token from any tradeapi.webullbroker.com request. Paste into the form. Tokens live in process memory only — never written to disk. Positions/orders/account poll every 5s.
view.tutorial.trading.paper: Simulated execution against the same quote pipeline as live. Order types: market, limit, stop, stop-limit. Reset starting cash anytime.
view.tutorial.trading.sizing: Three modes: Kelly fraction (from your historical win-rate), fixed-fractional (% of equity per trade), R-based (max loss in dollars / stop distance). Correlation-aware: caps concentration when symbols co-move.
view.tutorial.journal.journal: Per-trade, daily, and general notes. Each scope has its own templates editable under <button data-i18n="view.tutorial.btn.settings" class="link" data-go="settings">Settings</button> → Notes Templates.
view.tutorial.journal.ai: GPT-assisted post-mortem on individual trades. Requires an OpenAI key in <button data-i18n="view.tutorial.btn.ai_settings" class="link" data-go="ai">AI settings</button>. Output is cached so re-opening a trade is instant.
view.tutorial.journal.reviews: Forced reflection on |R| ≥ 2 trades. Tracks plan-quality, execution-quality, emotion. Surfaces "reviews needed" count on the dashboard.
view.tutorial.research.dark_pool: Off-exchange % print rankings and FINRA Reg-SHO daily short volume. Use to flag squeezable setups.
view.tutorial.strategy.backtest: stryke-JIT backtester. Define entry/exit on any indicator combination. Save presets, fork community presets, share via slug.
view.tutorial.data.quotes: Quotes / fundamentals / charts: Yahoo Finance v8/v10. Cached 60s per symbol in Postgres.
view.tutorial.data.live_ticks: Live ticks: Finnhub WebSocket. Free tier = 25 syms / connection; chunked automatically.
view.tutorial.data.sentiment: Sentiment: Reddit r/wallstreetbets JSON, StockTwits public stream.
view.tutorial.data.webull: Webull: session tokens you paste from your own browser. Read-only.
view.tutorial.rg.beginner: Beginner — 7 rules. 1% max per trade, 3% per day, 3 consec losses stop you, 15-min cool-down, 25% max position, plan + stop required. Strictest.
view.tutorial.rg.intermediate: Intermediate — 5 rules. Same per-trade cap, 5% daily, 4 streak, 5-min cool-down, stop required (plan optional).
view.tutorial.rg.aggressive: Aggressive — 2 rules. Daily-loss cap + cool-down only. Assumes you manage per-trade risk yourself.
view.tutorial.rg.tune: Tune — toggle individual rules off via the checkbox column. Delete with the link. Add custom rules with the form below the list.
view.tutorial.rg.dry_run: Dry-run a hypothetical trade in the bottom panel to see exactly what the gate would say. Same call the new-trade form makes on submit.
view.tutorial.rg.compliance: Compliance snapshot at top — synthetic probe that surfaces which rules would fire on ANY entry right now (max daily loss already hit, in cool-down after the last loss, etc).
view.tutorial.rg.paper: Paper trades also gate. Paper trading is where you build the habit; if rules only fired live, you'd practice rule-breaking.
view.tutorial.rg.webhook: Webhook on Block. Every Block-severity veto fires to every enabled webhook (Discord / Slack / generic) so you get a public record of every rule save.
view.tutorial.wf.pre_market: Pre-market (06:00–09:25 ET). Open to scan overnight movers. Open for fresh 8-K / press releases on watchlist symbols. Check for today's data drops.
view.tutorial.wf.open: Open (09:30–09:45 ET). Open with your gap watchlist. Open in a side window — T1 / T12 halts on small-caps are squeeze/dump candidates.
view.tutorial.wf.intraday: Intraday. Plan trades in before entry. Use to compute share count from your max-loss-R. Watch the entry on . Track open P/L on .
view.tutorial.wf.post: Post-market (16:00+). Run rollup. Write per-trade notes in . Any trade with |R| ≥ 2 gets a . Daily journal includes mood rating for .
view.tutorial.wf.weekly: Weekly. Run → exit efficiency + R-distribution. Re-evaluate which setups are paying. Update .
view.tutorial.trouble.launch_fail: App fails to launch with "pg_ctl: another server might be running": An orphan Postgres from a hard kill. Cleanup runs automatically on next launch — just re-open. If it persists, kill any stray postgres process owned by you.
view.tutorial.trouble.loading_forever: Widgets show "loading…" forever: Check ~/Library/Application Support/traderview/traderview.log — look for pool timed out (DB pool exhausted) or ERROR lines.
view.tutorial.trouble.ws_silent: WebSocket-driven views (Halts / Live Scanner / Catalysts / Webull) silent: The connect status dot (next to the view title) turns green on connect, red on error, gray on disconnected. Disconnected auto-reconnects in 4s.
view.tutorial.trouble.voice_silent: Voice alerts silent: Voice toggle is per-view. Browser SpeechSynthesis is throttled by macOS; if no voice fires for several events in a row, switch the system voice in System Settings → Accessibility → Spoken Content.
view.tutorial.trouble.stale: Premarket / markets data 60s stale: That's the in-process cache TTL. Yahoo throttles aggressive polling; restart to force-fresh.

view.scanners.preset.high_range_no_chang: HOD+LOD>5% + |change|<0.5 + rel_vol≥1 — wide range, zero net change
view.scanners.preset.low_range_no_change: HOD+LOD<1% + |change|<0.5 — very tight range with no net change
view.scanners.preset.low_volume_up_day_s: change 1-3 + rel_vol<0.5 — modest up day on dry volume (no participation)
view.scanners.preset.low_volume_down_day: change -3 to -1 + rel_vol<0.5 — modest down day on dry volume (no panic)
view.scanners.preset.hi_vol_up_day_no_ho: change 1-2 + rel_vol≥2 + HOD>0.5 — up day on heavy vol but failed to push HOD
view.scanners.preset.hi_vol_down_day_no_: change -2 to -1 + rel_vol≥2 + LOD>0.5 — down day on heavy vol but failed to push LOD
view.scanners.preset.gap_up_fade_to_flat: gap>2 + |change|<0.5 — gapped up overnight but unchanged on the day (full fade)
view.scanners.preset.gap_down_reclaim_to: gap<-2 + |change|<0.5 — gapped down overnight but unchanged on the day (full reclaim)
view.scanners.preset.gap_up_held_green.d: gap>2 + change>gap + rel_vol≥1 — gap held + extended on participation (continuation squeeze)
view.scanners.preset.gap_down_held_red.d: gap<-2 + change<gap + rel_vol≥1 — gap-down extended lower on participation (continuation squeeze)
view.scanners.preset.gap_up_half_fade.de: gap>2 + 0<change<gap*0.5 — gap-up faded to half its overnight move
view.scanners.preset.gap_down_half_recla: gap<-2 + gap*0.5<change<0 — gap-down reclaimed half its overnight move
view.scanners.preset.gap_and_go_xl.label: Gap And Go XL
view.scanners.preset.gap_and_go_xl.desc: gap>3 + change>5 + rel_vol≥2 — extra-large gap-and-go (strong gap + strong day + heavy vol)
view.scanners.preset.gap_and_crash_xl.de: gap<-3 + change<-5 + rel_vol≥2 — extra-large gap-and-crash (strong gap-down + strong red + heavy vol)
gap>1 + change<-1 — gapped up overnight but day closed red (failed open)
view.scanners.preset.gap_down_but_day_gr: gap<-1 + change>1 — gapped down overnight but day closed green (reversal)
view.scanners.preset.gap_up_flush_on_vol: gap>2 + change<-2 + rel_vol≥2 — failed open with heavy participation (distribution)
view.scanners.preset.gap_down_reversal_o: gap<-2 + change>2 + rel_vol≥2 — gap-down reversal with heavy participation (accumulation)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_top_decile_h: year_high>-10 + rel_vol≥2 — within 10% of 52w high on heavy volume (breakout candidate)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_bottom_decil: year_low<10 + rel_vol≥2 — within 10% of 52w low on heavy volume (capitulation candidate)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_top_decile_d: year_high>-10 + rel_vol<0.5 — within 10% of 52w high but dry volume (no demand at the highs)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_bottom_decil: year_low<10 + rel_vol<0.5 — within 10% of 52w low but dry volume (no panic at the lows)
year_high≥0 + change≥1 — printed a new 52w high and closed green (continuation)
view.scanners.preset.new_low_red_day.des: year_low≤0 + change≤-1 — printed a new 52w low and closed red (continuation)
view.scanners.preset.new_high_red_day.de: year_high≥0 + change≤-1 — printed a new 52w high then reversed red (failed breakout)
view.scanners.preset.new_low_green_day.d: year_low≤0 + change≥1 — printed a new 52w low then reversed green (failed breakdown)
view.scanners.preset.new_high_on_hot_vol: year_high≥0 + rel_vol≥3 — new 52w high on ≥3× volume (institutional accumulation)
year_low≤0 + rel_vol≥3 — new 52w low on ≥3× volume (institutional distribution)
year_high>-3 + HOD+LOD<1.5 + rel_vol<1 — tight range near 52w high on light vol (coiled spring up)
view.scanners.preset.quiet_near_the_bott: year_low<3 + HOD+LOD<1.5 + rel_vol<1 — tight range near 52w low on light vol (coiled spring down)
year_high>-3 + HOD+LOD>4 + rel_vol≥2 — wide range near 52w high on heavy vol (battle for the top)
view.scanners.preset.noisy_near_the_bott: year_low<3 + HOD+LOD>4 + rel_vol≥2 — wide range near 52w low on heavy vol (battle for the bottom)
view.scanners.preset.mid_range_chop_hot_: HOD,LOD each 1-3 + rel_vol≥2 — equidistant mid-range on heavy vol (indecision squeeze)
view.scanners.preset.mid_range_chop_dry_: HOD,LOD each 1-3 + rel_vol<0.5 — equidistant mid-range on dry vol (range-bound digestion)
view.scanners.preset.close_near_hod_no_b: |HOD|<0.5 + change<1 — closed within 0.5% of HOD but day stayed muted (failed thrust)
view.scanners.preset.close_near_lod_no_b: |LOD|<0.5 + change>-1 — closed within 0.5% of LOD but day stayed muted (failed flush)
view.scanners.preset.close_near_hod_stro: |HOD|<0.5 + change>3 — closed at HOD AND day up >3% (full-send breakout)
view.scanners.preset.close_near_lod_weak: |LOD|<0.5 + change<-3 — closed at LOD AND day down >3% (full-send breakdown)
view.scanners.preset.inside_range_no_vol: HOD+LOD<2 + rel_vol<0.5 — tight inside range on dry vol (NR7-style squeeze)
view.scanners.preset.outside_range_on_vo: HOD+LOD>6 + rel_vol≥2 — wide outside range on heavy vol (volatility expansion)
view.scanners.preset.up_day_lower_high.d: change>1 + |HOD|>1 + |LOD|<1 — green day but failed to push HOD (capped advance)
view.scanners.preset.down_day_higher_low: change<-1 + |LOD|>1 + |HOD|<1 — red day but failed to push LOD (cushioned decline)
view.scanners.preset.strong_day_balanced: change>3 + |HOD|<1 + |LOD|<1 — strong day with both ends touched (impulsive breakout)
view.scanners.preset.weak_day_balanced_r: change<-3 + |HOD|<1 + |LOD|<1 — weak day with both ends touched (impulsive breakdown)
view.scanners.preset.channel_ride_up.des: change>1 + day_pct>0 + |HOD|<0.5 + |LOD|>3 — close at HOD with LOD far away (one-side day up)
view.scanners.preset.channel_ride_down.d: change<-1 + day_pct<0 + |LOD|<0.5 + |HOD|>3 — close at LOD with HOD far away (one-side day down)
view.scanners.preset.pullback_in_uptrend: year_high>-15 + -3<change<0 + rel_vol<1 — minor pullback in uptrend on light vol (continuation buy zone)
view.scanners.preset.bounce_in_downtrend: year_low<15 + 0<change<3 + rel_vol<1 — minor bounce in downtrend on light vol (continuation short zone)
view.scanners.preset.deep_pullback_in_up: year_high>-25 + -10<change<-3 + rel_vol≥2 — deep pullback in uptrend on heavy vol (capitulation entry zone)
view.scanners.preset.deep_bounce_in_down: year_low<25 + 3<change<10 + rel_vol≥2 — deep bounce in downtrend on heavy vol (rip-and-reject entry zone)
view.scanners.preset.tight_above_mid_str: HOD+LOD<1.5 + day_pct>0 + change>0.5 — tight range above mid (coiled spring; bullish bias)
view.scanners.preset.tight_below_mid_wea: HOD+LOD<1.5 + day_pct<0 + change<-0.5 — tight range below mid (coiled spring; bearish bias)
view.scanners.preset.hot_vol_no_move_at_: Hot Vol, No Move at 52w High
year_high>-5 + |change|<0.5 + rel_vol≥2 — heavy churn at 52w high with no net move (distribution candidate)
Hot Vol, No Move at 52w Low
year_low<5 + |change|<0.5 + rel_vol≥2 — heavy churn at 52w low with no net move (accumulation candidate)
view.scanners.preset.big_up_gap_inside_d: gap>3 + |HOD|<0.5 + |LOD|<0.5 + 1<change<3 — big gap-up but contained inside-day (consolidation after thrust)
view.scanners.preset.big_down_gap_inside: gap<-3 + |HOD|<0.5 + |LOD|<0.5 + -3<change<-1 — big gap-down but contained inside-day (consolidation after thrust)
view.scanners.preset.steady_up_dry_vol.d: 0.5<change<2 + day_pct>0 + rel_vol<0.7 — steady-up day on light vol (low-conviction drift up)
view.scanners.preset.steady_down_dry_vol: -2<change<-0.5 + day_pct<0 + rel_vol<0.7 — steady-down day on light vol (low-conviction drift down)
view.scanners.preset.impulsive_up_hot_vo: 2<change<5 + day_pct>0 + rel_vol≥1.5 — impulsive up day on heavy vol (initiative buying)
view.scanners.preset.impulsive_down_hot_: -5<change<-2 + day_pct<0 + rel_vol≥1.5 — impulsive down day on heavy vol (initiative selling)
view.scanners.preset.parabolic_up.label: Parabolic Up
view.scanners.preset.parabolic_up.desc: change>10 + rel_vol≥3 + |HOD|<0.5 — parabolic up: >10% on ≥3× vol closing at HOD (blow-off candidate)
view.scanners.preset.parabolic_down.desc: change<-10 + rel_vol≥3 + |LOD|<0.5 — parabolic down: <-10% on ≥3× vol closing at LOD (panic candidate)
view.scanners.preset.blow_off_top.label: Blow-Off Top
view.scanners.preset.blow_off_top.desc: change>5 + rel_vol≥5 + year_high>-2 — extreme vol+move at 52w high (climactic top candidate)
view.scanners.preset.selling_climax_bott: change<-5 + rel_vol≥5 + year_low<2 — extreme vol+move at 52w low (selling climax candidate)
view.scanners.preset.up_day_gap_only_mov: 1<change<3 + |change-gap|<0.3 + rel_vol<1 — entire change came from overnight gap; flat day after
view.scanners.preset.down_day_gap_only_m: -3<change<-1 + |change-gap|<0.3 + rel_vol<1 — entire decline came from overnight gap-down; flat day after
view.scanners.preset.intraday_only_green: change>1 + |gap|<0.3 + rel_vol≥1 — flat open, intraday all-the-work green day (initiative buying continuation)
view.scanners.preset.intraday_only_red_d: change<-1 + |gap|<0.3 + rel_vol≥1 — flat open, intraday all-the-work red day (initiative selling continuation)
view.scanners.preset.reversal_up_from_op: gap<-1 + change>0 + rel_vol≥1.5 + |HOD|<0.5 — gap-down reversed + closed at HOD on heavy vol (powerful reclaim)
view.scanners.preset.reversal_down_from_: gap>1 + change<0 + rel_vol≥1.5 + |LOD|<0.5 — gap-up reversed + closed at LOD on heavy vol (powerful failure)
view.scanners.preset.trend_day_up.label: Trend Day Up
view.scanners.preset.trend_day_up.desc: change>2 + day_pct>1 + rel_vol≥1.2 + |HOD|<0.5 + |LOD|>2 — opened low, closed near HOD on heavy vol
view.scanners.preset.trend_day_down.desc: change<-2 + day_pct<-1 + rel_vol≥1.2 + |LOD|<0.5 + |HOD|>2 — opened high, closed near LOD on heavy vol
view.scanners.preset.double_bottom_candi: year_low<5 + |HOD|>1 + |LOD|<0.5 + change>0 — touched 52w-low zone but closed higher (double-bottom candidate)
view.scanners.preset.double_top_candidat: year_high>-5 + |LOD|>1 + |HOD|<0.5 + change<0 — touched 52w-high zone but closed lower (double-top candidate)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_mid_zone.des: -60<year_high<-40 + 40<year_low<60 — mid-zone of 52w range (no extreme positioning; chop bias)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_range_breako: year_high≥0 + change>2 + rel_vol≥2 — broke above 52w high range on heavy vol (range-breakout trigger)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_range_breakd: year_low≤0 + change<-2 + rel_vol≥2 — broke below 52w low range on heavy vol (range-breakdown trigger)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_tight_coil.d: -10≤year_high≤-5 + 5≤year_low≤10 + HOD+LOD<2 — coiled mid-high zone on tight range (decision-zone setup)
HOD+LOD<3 + |change|<0.5 + 0.7≤rel_vol≤1.3 — balanced tight range with avg vol (symmetric triangle wait)
view.scanners.preset.narrowing_range_on_: |HOD|,|LOD| each 0.5-2 + |change|<0.3 — both wicks small + no net move (narrowing-range setup)
view.scanners.preset.gap_too_far_big_pul: gap>4 + change<gap-3 — gapped up but pulled back >3% from the gap (over-extended fade)
view.scanners.preset.gap_too_far_big_bou: gap<-4 + change>gap+3 — gapped down but bounced >3% off the gap (over-extended bounce)
view.scanners.preset.chain_breakout_leve: |HOD|<0.3 + |LOD|>2 + change>1 — close at HOD with broad day-range (breakout above prior level)
view.scanners.preset.chain_breakdown_lev: |LOD|<0.3 + |HOD|>2 + change<-1 — close at LOD with broad day-range (breakdown below prior level)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_range_pos_to: year_high>-20 + year_low>30 — position in top half of 52w range (bullish positioning)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_range_pos_bo: year_high<-50 + year_low<30 — position in bottom half of 52w range (bearish positioning)
view.scanners.preset.high_range_high_vol: HOD+LOD>4 + change>3 + rel_vol≥1.5 — wide-range strong up day on heavy vol (initiative buying day)
HOD+LOD>4 + change<-3 + rel_vol≥1.5 — wide-range weak day on heavy vol (initiative selling day)
view.scanners.preset.low_range_low_vol_n: HOD+LOD<1.5 + |change|<0.5 + rel_vol<0.7 — quiet, tight, flat day (balance / observation)
view.scanners.preset.avg_range_avg_vol_n: 2≤HOD+LOD≤4 + |change|<0.5 + 0.8≤rel_vol≤1.2 — average range/vol with no net move (no-edge day)
view.scanners.preset.failed_breakout_hig: year_high>-1 + |HOD|>1 + change<-1 + rel_vol≥1.5 — touched/exceeded 52w high then closed -1% lower on heavy vol
view.scanners.preset.failed_breakdown_lo: year_low<1 + |LOD|>1 + change>1 + rel_vol≥1.5 — touched/exceeded 52w low then closed +1% higher on heavy vol
view.scanners.preset.hot_vol_hot_gap.des: |gap|>2 + rel_vol≥2 — heavy-volume gap day (institutional positioning before open)
view.scanners.preset.dry_vol_dry_gap.des: |gap|<0.5 + rel_vol<0.5 — flat gap on dry vol (no overnight positioning + no day participation)
year_high>-10 + change>5 + rel_vol≥2 — strong push into top decile on heavy vol (continuation buyers)
view.scanners.preset.outer_edge_push_dow: year_low<10 + change<-5 + rel_vol≥2 — strong push into bottom decile on heavy vol (continuation sellers)
view.scanners.preset.middle_zone_up_drif: -50≤year_high≤-20 + 20≤year_low≤50 + change>0.5 + rel_vol<1 — mid-zone drift up on light vol (no-conviction continuation up)
view.scanners.preset.middle_zone_down_dr: -50≤year_high≤-20 + 20≤year_low≤50 + change<-0.5 + rel_vol<1 — mid-zone drift down on light vol (no-conviction continuation down)
view.scanners.preset.middle_zone_hot_vol: -50≤year_high≤-20 + change>2 + rel_vol≥2 — mid-zone breakout up on heavy vol (range-exit conviction)
20≤year_low≤50 + change<-2 + rel_vol≥2 — mid-zone breakdown on heavy vol (range-exit conviction)
view.scanners.preset.gap_up_small_but_ho: 0.5≤gap≤1.5 + rel_vol≥2 — small overnight gap-up on heavy vol (early-positioning signal)
view.scanners.preset.gap_down_small_but_: -1.5≤gap≤-0.5 + rel_vol≥2 — small overnight gap-down on heavy vol (early-positioning signal)
view.scanners.preset.gap_up_medium_neutr: 1.5≤gap≤3 + |change|<0.5 + rel_vol<1 — medium gap-up but flat day on light vol (gap-and-stall)
view.scanners.preset.gap_down_medium_neu: -3≤gap≤-1.5 + |change|<0.5 + rel_vol<1 — medium gap-down but flat day on light vol (gap-and-stall)
view.scanners.preset.hod_reclaim_after_f: change>0 + |HOD|<0.5 + |LOD|>2 + rel_vol≥1.5 — closed at HOD after touching deep LOD on heavy vol (V-bottom intraday)
view.scanners.preset.lod_fail_after_push: change<0 + |LOD|<0.5 + |HOD|>2 + rel_vol≥1.5 — closed at LOD after touching distant HOD on heavy vol (failed-push intraday)
view.scanners.preset.hod_reclaim_from_fl: |gap|<0.5 + |HOD|<0.5 + change>1 — flat open then closed at HOD with positive change (organic up-day climb)
view.scanners.preset.lod_fail_from_flat_: |gap|<0.5 + |LOD|<0.5 + change<-1 — flat open then closed at LOD with negative change (organic down-day slide)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_top_boundary: -1≤year_high≤0 + change<-0.5 — touched 52w high boundary but closed lower (rejection from top boundary)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_bottom_bound: 0≤year_low≤1 + change>0.5 — touched 52w low boundary but closed higher (rejection from bottom boundary)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_top_boundary: -1≤year_high≤0 + change>0.5 — touched 52w high boundary and closed higher (acceptance above prior high)
view.scanners.preset.pct52w_bottom_bound: 0≤year_low≤1 + change<-0.5 — touched 52w low boundary and closed lower (acceptance below prior low)
view.scanners.preset.up_from_bottom_spri: year_low<10 + change>5 + rel_vol≥2 — strong rally up from 52w lows on heavy vol (spring reversal)
view.scanners.preset.down_from_top_upthr: year_high>-10 + change<-5 + rel_vol≥2 — strong sell-off from 52w highs on heavy vol (upthrust reversal)
view.scanners.preset.up_thrust_bar_rejec: |HOD|>3 + |LOD|<0.5 + change<-1 + rel_vol≥2 — wick high then closed near LOD on heavy vol (upthrust bar)
view.scanners.preset.down_thrust_bar_rej: |LOD|>3 + |HOD|<0.5 + change>1 + rel_vol≥2 — wick low then closed near HOD on heavy vol (spring bar)
view.scanners.preset.exhaustion_top_wide: year_high>-5 + |HOD|>5 + |LOD|<0.5 + change>5 + rel_vol≥3 — extreme range close-at-HOD into prior high (exhaustion top)
view.scanners.preset.exhaustion_bottom_w: year_low<5 + |LOD|>5 + |HOD|<0.5 + change<-5 + rel_vol≥3 — extreme range close-at-LOD into prior low (exhaustion bottom)
view.scanners.preset.up_trend_day_wide_r: |HOD|<0.3 + |LOD|>5 + change>3 + rel_vol≥2 — wide-range up day; close at HOD on heavy vol (continuation buyers)
view.scanners.preset.down_trend_day_wide: |LOD|<0.3 + |HOD|>5 + change<-3 + rel_vol≥2 — wide-range down day; close at LOD on heavy vol (continuation sellers)
view.scanners.preset.silent_spring_near_: year_low<5 + |change|<0.5 + rel_vol<0.5 + HOD+LOD<1.5 — flat tight bar at 52w low on dry vol (silent spring waiting)
view.scanners.preset.silent_up_thrust_ne: year_high>-5 + |change|<0.5 + rel_vol<0.5 + HOD+LOD<1.5 — flat tight bar at 52w high on dry vol (silent upthrust waiting)
view.scanners.preset.gap_strong_day_open: 1≤gap≤3 + change>4 + rel_vol≥2 — small-to-medium gap + strong day on heavy vol (gap held + accelerated)
view.scanners.preset.gap_weak_day_open_p: -3≤gap≤-1 + change<-4 + rel_vol≥2 — small-to-medium gap-down + weak day on heavy vol (gap held + accelerated)
view.scanners.preset.conviction_breakout: year_high≥0 + |HOD|<0.5 + change>3 + rel_vol≥2.5 — 52w-high broken + close at HOD + strong day + heavy vol
view.scanners.preset.conviction_breakdow: year_low≤0 + |LOD|<0.5 + change<-3 + rel_vol≥2.5 — 52w-low broken + close at LOD + weak day + heavy vol
view.scanners.preset.pullback_inside_tre: -20≤year_high≤-5 + -1≤change≤-0.2 + 0.7≤rel_vol≤1.3 — small pullback inside uptrend with avg vol (orderly continuation)
5≤year_low≤20 + 0.2≤change≤1 + 0.7≤rel_vol≤1.3 — small bounce inside downtrend with avg vol (orderly continuation)
view.scanners.preset.range_contraction_s: year_high>-5 + HOD+LOD<1 + rel_vol<0.5 + |change|<0.3 — extreme range contraction at 52w high (mega-squeeze coil)
year_low<5 + HOD+LOD<1 + rel_vol<0.5 + |change|<0.3 — extreme range contraction at 52w low (mega-squeeze coil)
view.scanners.preset.range_expansion_at_: year_high>-5 + HOD+LOD>6 + rel_vol≥2 + |change|<0.5 — wide-range churn at 52w high with no net move on heavy vol (distribution at top)
year_low<5 + HOD+LOD>6 + rel_vol≥2 + |change|<0.5 — wide-range churn at 52w low with no net move on heavy vol (accumulation at bottom)
view.scanners.preset.gap_inside_range_ba: |gap|<1 + HOD+LOD<2 + |change|<0.5 — flat gap + tight range + flat day (multi-day balance candidate)
view.scanners.preset.gap_inside_range_im: |gap|<1 + HOD+LOD>4 + |change|>2 + rel_vol≥1.5 — flat gap but wide impulsive day on heavy vol (intraday breakout from balance)
view.scanners.preset.one_wick_close_at_m: |HOD|>2 + |LOD|<0.5 + |change|<0.5 — long upper wick then closed flat (rejection from upper extreme)
|LOD|>2 + |HOD|<0.5 + |change|<0.5 — long lower wick then closed flat (rejection from lower extreme)
view.scanners.preset.upper_wick_green_da: |HOD|>2 + |LOD|<0.5 + change>1 — long upper wick + closed green (failed reversal; trend continuation)
view.scanners.preset.lower_wick_red_day_: |LOD|>2 + |HOD|<0.5 + change<-1 — long lower wick + closed red (failed reversal; trend continuation)
view.scanners.preset.inside_bar_tight_at: HOD+LOD<1 + |change|<0.2 + rel_vol<0.8 — extremely tight inside bar at mid (NR4 / NR7 silent compression)
view.scanners.preset.outside_bar_volume_: |HOD|>3 + |LOD|>3 + rel_vol≥2 + |change|<0.5 — outside-bar both extremes touched on heavy vol with no net move
view.scanners.preset.leading_up_day_ligh: change>2 + rel_vol<0.7 + |HOD|<0.5 — strong up close on light vol (leadership without participation; suspect quality)
view.scanners.preset.leading_down_day_li: change<-2 + rel_vol<0.7 + |LOD|<0.5 — strong down close on light vol (leadership without participation; suspect quality)
view.scanners.preset.small_change_on_vol: year_high>-3 + 0.5≤|change|≤1.5 + rel_vol≥1.5 — modest move at 52w high on above-avg vol (top consolidation / distribution prep)
year_low<3 + 0.5≤|change|≤1.5 + rel_vol≥1.5 — modest move at 52w low on above-avg vol (bottom consolidation / accumulation prep)
view.scanners.preset.big_gap_big_vol_big: Big Gap + Big Vol + Big Day
|gap|>3 + |change|>5 + rel_vol≥3 — extreme gap + extreme day + extreme vol (all-in conviction)
view.scanners.preset.big_gap_no_follow_t: |gap|>3 + |change|<1 + rel_vol<1 — extreme gap + flat day + dry vol (failed positioning; no follow-through)
view.scanners.preset.confluence_long_set: |gap|<0.5 + 5≤year_low≤15 + 0.5≤change≤1.5 + rel_vol≥1.2 — flat-open near 52w low + minor green move + above-avg vol (long setup)
view.scanners.preset.confluence_short_se: |gap|<0.5 + -15≤year_high≤-5 + -1.5≤change≤-0.5 + rel_vol≥1.2 — flat-open near 52w high + minor red move + above-avg vol (short setup)
view.scanners.preset.no_extreme_day.desc: -40≤year_high≤-10 + 10≤year_low≤40 + |change|<0.5 — middle-of-range flat day (no extreme positioning; no edge)
view.scanners.preset.accelerating_up_tre: change>1 + day_pct>0 + year_high>-5 + rel_vol>1 — pushing harder near 52w high on above-avg vol (accelerating uptrend)
view.scanners.preset.accelerating_down_t: change<-1 + day_pct<0 + year_low<5 + rel_vol>1 — pushing harder near 52w low on above-avg vol (accelerating downtrend)
view.scanners.preset.divergence_push_fro: year_high>-3 + change<-1 + rel_vol<0.8 — touched 52w high but closed red on light vol (divergence reject from top)
year_low<3 + change>1 + rel_vol<0.8 — touched 52w low but closed green on light vol (divergence reject from bottom)
view.scanners.preset.price_flat_vol_hot_: HOD+LOD<2 + day_pct>0 + rel_vol≥1.5 + |change|<0.3 — flat price + above-mid + heavy vol (silent accumulation)

view.scanners.preset.year52_low_with_ran: Near 52w Low + Tight Range + Flat Change (Coil at the Bottom)

view.scanners.preset.red_day_sub_optimal: Red Day + Significant Bounce from LOD (Sub-optimal Close on Down Day)

view.scanners.preset.wide_range_no_vol_f: Wide Range + Dry Vol + Flat Change (Fake-liquidity Range Exploration)

view.trade_plan_checklist.validate.thesi: thesis must be a string (can be empty)
view.optimal_f.validate.need_loser: need at least one losing trade (Vince formula requires worst_loss > 0)
view.pair_trade.validate.length_mismatch: y and x must be the same length (got {yLen} vs {xLen})
view.pair_trade.validate.exit_lt_entry: exit_z must be < entry_z (exit only when z mean-reverts inside entry band)
view.drawdown_throttle.validate.tiers_so: tiers must be sorted ascending by min_dd
view.daily_loss_limit.validate.threshold: thresholds must satisfy warning < cut_size ≤ kill
view.almgren_chriss.validate.total_share: total_shares must be a non-zero finite number
view.alligator.validate.need_bars: need at least 21 bars (13 SMMA + 8 jaw shift)
view.active_share.validate.weight_symbol: weights[{i}].symbol must be a non-empty string
view.var_calculator.validate.need_20: need at least 20 returns for a meaningful VaR estimate
view.var_calculator.validate.constant: returns are constant — VaR is undefined
view.iv_solver.validate.no_arb_band: market_price {price} is outside the no-arb band [{lower}, {upper}] — IV cannot exist
view.microprice.validate.need_size: at least one side must have size
view.iv_rank.validate.history_min: history must have at least 10 observations
view.iv_rank.validate.history_finite: history must contain only non-negative finite values
view.iv_rank.note.agree: rank and percentile agree closely — trust either
view.iv_rank.note.mild: mild divergence between rank and percentile
view.iv_rank.note.diverge: rank and percentile diverge ≥20pts — series is skewed, prefer percentile
view.iv_backtest.validate.implied_bps: implied_move_pct looks like raw bps — enter as %, e.g. 5.5 for 5.5%
view.iv_backtest.validate.realized_finit: realized history must contain only finite values

view.alerts.empty_kind_chart: No alert rules to chart by kind.
view.alerts.tip.symbol: Ticker the alert watches; uppercased on submit

view.alerts.tip.sound: Audio cue played when the rule fires; voice plays after the sound
view.alerts.tip.voice_text: Optional TTS phrase spoken on fire; leave blank for the default

view.alerts.hint.audio_voice_alerts_on_p: Audio + voice alerts on price / % / volume / signal triggers. Polls every 60s.
view.alligator.hint.mouth_width: |lips − jaw| over time: wide = trending (alligator eating); near zero = sleeping. Use as a regime-strength signal independent of direction.
view.alligator.hint.cyan_median_price_bl:
Cyan = median price. Blue = jaw (13-SMMA, +8 shift).
                Red = teeth (8-SMMA, +5 shift). Green = lips (5-SMMA, +3 shift).
                Lines fan out (green above red above blue) = hunting up. Reverse stack =
                hunting down. Intertwined = sleeping.
view.alligator.hint.one_cell_per_bar_gre:
One cell per bar — green up, red down, grey sleeping.
                Visualizes regime transitions across the full series at a glance.
view.almgren_chriss.hint.controls_how_fr:
κ = √(λσ²/η) controls how front-loaded the trajectory is. λ→0 collapses to TWAP
                (uniform liquidation, minimum impact, maximum timing risk). λ→∞ front-loads
                aggressively, eating impact cost to dump timing risk. The frontier shows the
                cost/variance tradeoff over a geometric λ ladder centred on your chosen λ.
view.almgren_chriss.hint.each_dot_is_a_s:
Each dot is a solve at one λ. The dashed white marker is
                your currently selected λ. Move down-right = more patient. Up-left =
                more urgent.
view.american_option.vol_chart_caption: European BS price across σ ∈ [0.05, 1.0] at fixed input spot, plus a single LSMC point at the input σ.
view.american_option.tip.kind: Call or Put; early-exercise premium typically shows up on puts (no-div spot)
view.american_option.tip.sigma: Volatility (decimal); enters the GBM process for path generation
view.american_option.tip.steps: Time steps per path; more = finer early-exercise grid
view.american_option.tip.paths: Monte Carlo paths; standard error shrinks as 1/√N
view.american_option.tip.price: Run LSMC; compare against Black-Scholes European reference
view.american_option.hint.longstaff_schw:
Longstaff-Schwartz 2001 regression-Monte-Carlo. Larger paths shrink the standard
                error as 1/√N. Default 5,000 paths gives ~1% SE for at-the-money options.

view.backtest.tip.sharpe_daily: Daily-bar Sharpe — mean daily return ÷ stdev, risk-free assumed 0
view.backtest.tip.sharpe_ann: Daily Sharpe × √252 — convention for annualizing daily returns
view.backtest.tip.pct_in_market: Fraction of bars holding a position; lower = idle capital, higher = exposure
view.backtest.hint.bar_by_bar_over_cache:

view.bocpd.h2.expected_run_length_bars_s: Expected run length (bars since last change)
view.bocpd.tip.hazard: Per-bar prior on regime change (0.0001–0.5); lower = sharper detections
view.bocpd.tip.threshold: Min posterior P to flag as a change point (0–1); 0 shows every bar
view.bocpd.tip.top_k: Max change points to list (1–20), sorted by probability desc
view.bocpd.tip.detect: Run Adams-MacKay BOCPD; render returns + CP overlay + run-length chart
view.bocpd.tip.returns: Return series (decimal); one per token; # comments OK
view.bocpd.hint.hazard_the_per_bar_prior:
Hazard = the per-bar prior probability that the underlying distribution
                changes. Lower hazard → fewer, sharper detections. The threshold filters
                the displayed change points by posterior probability — change to 0 to see
                every bar's score.
view.bocpd.hint.returns_on_left_axis_gre:
Returns on left axis (grey). Change-point probability on right axis (red);
                values near 1 mark bars where BOCPD is confident the regime shifted.
view.bocpd.hint.sawtooth_pattern_climbs_:
Sawtooth pattern: climbs by 1 each calm bar, drops to ~0 at every detected
                change point. Long flat-rising stretches = stable regime; frequent resets =
                noisy regime with many detected breaks.
view.breadth.empty_value_chart: No breadth indicators to chart values.
view.breadth.hint.intraday_tape_regime_n:
Intraday tape regime: NYSE TICK (instantaneous up-tick count),
            TRIN (Arms Index — volume bias), Advance-Decline issues, Up-Down volume,
            CBOE Put-Call ratio. Composite score combines all five into a -100..+100
            regime gauge. Polls every 60s.
view.breadth.hint.put_call_is_a_contrari: * Put-Call is a contrarian indicator at extremes — very low PCR = complacency (often near tops), very high PCR = fear (often near bottoms).
view.breadth.hint.no_breadth_tickers_ret: No breadth tickers returned data — try in market hours.

SEC EDGAR (every 6s) + Business Wire / PR Newswire / GlobeNewswire /
            AccessWire (every 30s). Tickers auto-extracted via NER.
            Filter for your watchlist on the left; voice alerts fire when
            any of those symbols appear in a fresh filing or press release.

                only structural reversals.
view.chandelier_stop.hint.cyan_close_yel:
Cyan = close. Yellow = chandelier stop (ratcheted). Red dot =
                trigger fired on this bar. Long stop trails BELOW price; short stop trails ABOVE.

view.choppiness.hint.formula_ci_100_log1: Formula: CI = 100 × log10(ΣTR / (max H − min L)) / log10(period). Default period 14. Reference bands: 61.8 (choppy line), 38.2 (trending line).
view.choppiness.hint.cyan_close_left_axi: Cyan = close (left axis). Yellow = CI (right axis 0–100). Red dashed = 61.8 (chop), green dashed = 38.2 (trend).

view.clusters_trade_features.hint.entry_: Entry minute is minutes from midnight (UTC) — e.g. 9:30am ET ≈ 870. Distance is normalized: entry/1440, hold/1440, R/5 so each dim contributes ~comparably.
view.clusters_trade_features.hint.each_d: Each dot is a trade. Color = cluster_id. X axis = entry minute of day (0–1440). Y = hold duration in minutes.

view.compare.hint.side_by_side_fundament:
Side-by-side fundamentals + multi-horizon returns + 252-bar relative-strength
            overlay (each line rebased to 100 at the window start). Up to 4 symbols, comma-separated.
            Best metric in each row is highlighted in green; worst in red.
view.compare.hint.no_cached_bars_for_any: no cached bars for any symbol — populate the prices cache first.
view.corr_matrix.h2.most_correlated_pair: Most correlated pairs (overlap risk if both long)
view.corr_matrix.hint.pairwise_pearson_c:
Pairwise Pearson correlation on log-returns of cached daily bars
            with intersected dates (pairs need ≥30 common observations to score). Red = strongly
            positive (moves together — overlap risk if both long), green = strongly negative
            (true diversifier), grey = uncorrelated. The diagonal is fixed at 1.
view.corr_matrix.hint.pick_a_watchlist_o: Pick a watchlist or paste symbols and compute.
view.cov_denoiser.empty_cum_chart: Cleaned spectrum has zero total variance.
view.cov_denoiser.hint.marchenko_pastur_:
Marchenko-Pastur clipping: eigenvalues inside the noise bulk are flattened
                to their average; only signal eigenvalues survive. Trace is preserved.
                Pipe the cleaned cov into the Portfolio Allocator for more robust weights
                than raw-sample-cov input.
view.cov_denoiser.hint.cyan_markers_orig:
Cyan markers = original sample eigenvalues (sorted descending).
                Red dashed line = λ_max bulk threshold. Anything above the line is
                "signal" (preserved); anything at or below is "noise" (replaced by the
                bulk average — visible as the orange flat segment in the cleaned series).
view.crypto.h2.from_ath_chart: Top-10 % from ATH — drawdown from cycle peak
view.crypto.hint.coingecko_rate_limit_hi: CoinGecko rate limit hit — retry in a minute.

                5-25 handle bars, ≤15% handle dip. Loosen depth/handle to surface near-misses
                during base-building.
view.cup_and_handle.hint.yellow_left_rim:
Yellow = left rim. Magenta = trough. Cyan = right rim. Orange =
                handle low. Green dashed = pivot (IBD buy-point = right-rim high).

Save named indicator + parameter combos (SMA, EMA, RSI, Bollinger,
            MACD). The Charts tab gets a multi-select to overlay any of them on the SVG cursor.
            Backend evaluates the chosen presets against cached bars and returns one series

view.cusum.h2.detector_state_chart: CUSUM detector state — G+ and G− vs threshold band

view.cusum.tip.series: One signed value per line (returns/vol-normalized series); # comments OK

view.cusum.tip.autofit: Compute mean + stdev from the series and fill the reference inputs
view.cusum.tip.mean: Baseline mean the CUSUM tracks deviations against
view.cusum.tip.sd: Reference stdev; threshold + slack are expressed as multiples of this
view.cusum.tip.thr: Trigger level in stdevs; lower = chatty detector, higher = laggy
view.cusum.tip.slk: Per-bar deduction to suppress noise; 0.5 × stdev is conventional

shortcut.cusum_autofit: Auto-fit reference mean + stdev from the series
view.cusum.hint.paste_one_signed_value_p:
Paste one signed value per line — typically log-returns or
                vol-normalized returns. Demo loads 200 bars where mean flips at bar 100.
view.cusum.hint.threshold_stdevs_referen:
threshold_stdevs × reference_stdev = trigger level. Slack subtracts a
                small amount each bar to suppress noise (typically 0.5 × stdev).
                A high threshold + zero slack = chatty detector. Low threshold + large
                slack = laggy but stable.
view.cusum.hint.cyan_the_series_itself_g:
Cyan = the series itself. Green dots = UP change-points
                (regime mean shifted higher). Red dots = DOWN change-points (mean shifted lower).
                Marker height = CUSUM value at the firing bar (how hard the threshold was busted).
view.cypher_pattern.h2.quality_chart: Per-match geometric quality (lower = closer to ideal Cypher)
view.cypher_pattern.empty_quality_chart: No Cypher matches to chart quality.
view.cypher_pattern.hint.cyan_pivot_pric:
Cyan = pivot price connecting line. Yellow dots = pivots. When
                a Cypher matches, X/A/B/C/D markers are highlighted on the first match's pivots.
