view.deflated_sharpe.tip.trials: How many strategies you tried; raises the √(2 ln N) noise floor
view.deflated_sharpe.tip.deflate: Compute deflated threshold + PSR for the current inputs
view.deflated_sharpe.tip.sweep: Recompute across a range of N_trials so you can see PSR fragility
view.deflated_sharpe.hint.cyan_observed_:
Cyan = observed SR. Magenta = deflated threshold (the noise floor).
                If cyan doesn't clearly exceed magenta you're inside the multiple-testing band.
view.deflated_sharpe.hint.psr_vs_n_trial:
PSR vs N_trials. Tells you how fragile your conclusion is —
                if PSR collapses past N = 100 you've overfit even by Bailey-LdP's
                generous benchmark.
view.demark_pivots.h2.distance_chart: Signed distance from spot to each level (R1 / pivot / S1)
view.demark_pivots.empty_distance_chart: Provide spot and a valid pivot to chart distances.
view.demark_pivots.hint.demark_s_x_base_:
DeMark's X-base formula switches on close-vs-open direction:
                bearish session → low-heavy X; bullish → high-heavy X; doji → close-heavy X.
                Pivot = X/4, R1 = X/2 − low, S1 = X/2 − high.
view.demark_pivots.hint.magenta_prior_oh:
Magenta = prior OHLC envelope. Yellow = pivot. Red dashed = R1.
                Green dashed = S1. Cyan = today's spot. Distance from price to nearest
                level frames the day's trade setup.
view.demarker.h2.regime_chart: Regime time-share (OB / neutral / OS bar counts)
view.demarker.empty_regime_chart: Compute DeMarker to chart regime time-share.
view.demarker.hint.bounded_0_1_0_70_over:
Bounded [0, 1]. ≥0.70 = overbought (setup for short / mean-reversion).
                ≤0.30 = oversold (setup for long / mean-reversion). Crossovers from neutral into
                an extreme region are surfaced as event alerts.
view.demarker.hint.cyan_demarker_red_das:
Cyan = DeMarker. Red dashed = 0.70 OB threshold. Green dashed =
                0.30 OS threshold. Yellow = 0.50 mid.
view.discipline.h2.rule_chart: Per-rule pass rate — which discipline rule is breaking most
view.discipline.hint.no_linked_plans_yet: No linked plans yet — create a trade plan in the Plans tab and link it to a trade.
view.disclosures.h2.kind_chart: Filing count by source kind (insider / Senate / House)
view.disclosures.empty_chart: No filings with $ amount to chart.
view.disclosures.empty_kind_chart: No filings to chart by kind.
view.disclosures.hint.polls_sec_edgar_fo:
Polls SEC EDGAR Form 4 + Senate eFD + House Clerk every 20 seconds.
            Sub-30s alerts vs Quiver Quant's ~5min. Configure watchers to fire
            audio + browser-push when your rule matches.
view.disclosures.hint.no_disclosures_cac: No disclosures cached yet — hit "Poll now" to fetch.
view.disclosures.th.name: Name
view.dividend_calendar.hint.pulls_per_sy:
Pulls per-symbol dividend data in parallel from the research backend.
                Symbols without dividend data (non-payers, ETFs, ADRs) are silently
                skipped. Past-dated ex-dates are filtered out.
view.drawdown_throttle.h2.equity_curve_r: Equity curve + rolling peak + underwater drawdown
view.drawdown_throttle.h2.ladder_chart: Tier ladder — size multiplier as a function of drawdown
view.drawdown_throttle.empty_ladder_char: Provide at least one tier to chart the ladder.
view.drawdown_throttle.hint.one_value_pe:
One value per line — most-recent last. Demo presets land in
                each tier so the trader sees the full throttle ladder in action.
view.drawdown_throttle.hint.cyan_equity_:
Cyan = equity. Yellow = rolling peak. Red = underwater
                (peak − current as a negative %). Active throttle tier highlighted in legend.
view.dtw.tip.a: Series A values; one per token; need not match B's length
view.dtw.tip.b: Series B values; one per token; DTW aligns A↔B regardless of length
view.dtw.tip.band: Sakoe-Chiba band radius (|i−j| ≤ r); 0 = unconstrained full DP
view.dtw.tip.warp: Run DTW; render overlay + warping path with diagonal reference
view.dtw.hint.sakoe_chiba_band_constrain:
Sakoe-Chiba band constrains the warping path so |i−j| ≤ radius. Set 0 to
                disable (full O(n·m) DP); set a small radius (~10-20% of series length) to
                speed up long series and avoid pathological cross-warps.
view.dtw.hint.cyan_optimal_alignment_fro:
Cyan: optimal alignment from DTW (A's index ↔ B's index). Dashed orange:
                diagonal reference (y = x). Departures from the diagonal indicate where DTW
                found one series leading or lagging the other.
view.earnings_cal.h2.reaction_chart: 1-day vs 5-day reaction — did the gap reaction foreshadow the week?
view.earnings_cal.tip.days: Days ahead to show in the calendar matrix (1–30)
view.earnings_cal.tip.back: Lookback window for the surprise leaderboard (1–365 days)
view.earnings_cal.tip.refresh: Re-fetch calendar + surprise data with the current filters
view.earnings_cal.tip.poll: Force a Yahoo poll now instead of waiting for the next 6h cycle
view.earnings_cal.hint.before_market_ope: ☀ before market open · 🌙 after market close · · timing unknown
view.earnings_iv.hint.no_earnings_inside: No earnings inside the horizon for your universe. Add some watchlist symbols and rerun.
view.earnings_iv.hint.long_short_p_l_is_: Long/Short P&L is per-quarter average return on $1 of premium across the historical sample.
view.earnings_iv.hint.no_historical_earn: No historical earnings moves in cached price bars yet — wait a moment for the prices fetcher.
view.economy.h2.category_chart: Event count by category — which macro themes dominate the horizon
view.economy.empty_category_chart: No events to chart by category.
view.economy.tip.horizon: Days from today to fetch; clamped 1–365 by the input
view.economy.tip.importance: Floor on event importance; Medium+ filters out routine releases
view.economy.tip.load: Fetch the economic calendar with the chosen filters
view.equity_forecast.h2.equity_fan_p5_p2: Equity fan (p5 / p25 / p50 / p75 / p95)
view.equity_forecast.h2.uncertainty_char: Forecast uncertainty growth — (p95 − p5) / p50 by trade #
view.equity_forecast.empty_chart: Run a forecast to chart percentile bands.
view.equity_forecast.empty_uncertainty_c: Run a forecast to chart uncertainty growth.
view.equity_forecast.tip.start: Starting equity in $; all final amounts compare against this
view.equity_forecast.tip.risk: Per-trade risk % (0.1–100); equity × risk = $ at risk each step
view.equity_forecast.tip.paths: Monte Carlo paths (100–50000); more = smoother percentile bands
view.equity_forecast.tip.ruin: % of start equity below which a path is marked 'ruined' and frozen
view.equity_forecast.tip.run: Bootstrap R-multiples from history; run paths; render fan + bands
view.execution_scheduler.h2.cumulative_c: Cumulative shares filled per algo — completion timing comparison
view.execution_scheduler.tip.total: Total shares to work across the volume curve
view.execution_scheduler.tip.rate: POV fraction (0.01–1.0) of each bar's expected volume to take
view.execution_scheduler.tip.schedule: Build TWAP / VWAP / POV slice schedules in parallel
view.execution_scheduler.tip.volume: Per-bar expected volumes; one value per token; comments OK
view.execution_scheduler.toast.done: Scheduled across {bars} bars — TWAP / VWAP / POV
view.execution_scheduler.hint.twap_slice:
TWAP slices equally across all bars in the curve. VWAP weights by expected
                volume. POV takes the participation-rate fraction of each bar — may fall short.
view.execution_scheduler.hint.three_seri: Three series, one per algo. Vertical bars show shares per bar.
view.expenses.empty_month_chart: No transactions to chart monthly trend.
view.exports.h2.cumulative_chart: Cumulative closed trades — running activity total
view.exports.empty_chart: No closed trades in the last 5 years to chart.
view.exports.empty_cum_chart: No closed trades in the last 5 years to chart cumulative.
view.exports.hint.no_account_selected_cr: No account selected. Create one on the Accounts tab first.
view.exports.hint.csv_downloads_use_rfc_:
CSV downloads use RFC 4180 quoting. The tax package opens a
            single-page printable HTML report in a new tab — save as PDF via your browser's
            print dialog (the embedded button auto-hides on print).
view.exports.hint.tax_package_pdf_opens_: Tax-package PDF: opens in a new tab, then ⌘P / Ctrl+P → "Save as PDF".
view.fear_greed.h2.deviation_chart: Component deviation from composite — which signals tilt the score
view.fear_greed.empty_dev_chart: No component scores to chart deviation.
view.fear_greed.hint.cnn_methodology_com:
CNN-methodology composite of seven risk-appetite signals.
            Score 0 = extreme fear, 100 = extreme greed. Each component is normalized
            to 0..100 and the average is taken. Refreshes every 90s.
view.fear_greed.hint.like_all_sentiment_:
Like all sentiment composites this is contrarian at the extremes —
                Extreme Fear historically aligns with intermediate-term lows, Extreme Greed with
                local tops. Use as a regime filter, not a timing signal.
view.fill_quality.h2.symbol_chart: Avg slippage (bps) by symbol — which names cost most
view.footprint.h2.cum_delta_chart: Cumulative delta across bars — running order-flow imbalance
view.footprint.empty_cum_chart: No bar deltas to chart cumulative.
view.forward_vol_curve.h2.variance_chart: Total variance σ²·t — must monotonically increase (no-arb)
view.forward_vol_curve.empty_variance_ch: Provide a term structure to chart variance.
view.forward_vol_curve.hint.variance_add:
Variance additivity: σ²·t accumulates linearly across non-overlapping windows.
                A negative back-end forward variance flags a calendar-arb violation.
view.forward_vol_curve.hint.cyan_line_sp:
Cyan line = spot IV at each tenor (interpolation purely cosmetic).
                Orange step = bootstrapped forward vol active over each tenor window.
                Red markers flag violation intervals.
view.fx_option.h2.delta_chart: Numerical delta vs spot — sigmoid through strike
view.fx_option.tip.kind: Call = right to buy foreign; Put = right to sell foreign
view.fx_option.tip.spot: Spot in domestic per 1 unit of foreign (e.g. EURUSD = USD per EUR)
view.fx_option.tip.strike: Strike in the same domestic/foreign convention as spot
view.fx_option.tip.rd: Domestic risk-free rate (decimal); discounts the strike
view.fx_option.tip.rf: Foreign risk-free rate (decimal); discounts the spot
view.fx_option.tip.sigma: FX volatility (decimal); enters the GBM for the spot path
view.fx_option.tip.price: Compute Garman-Kohlhagen price + greeks; redraw price-vs-spot
view.fx_option.hint.garman_kohlhagen_pri:
Garman-Kohlhagen price across ±20% of the input spot. The orange marker is
                the backend's response at the user's spot — should sit exactly on the curve
                (sanity check that the local closed-form matches the Rust module).
view.goal_tracker.h2.dd_chart: Running drawdown — peak-to-current % with kill-switch cap
view.goal_tracker.empty_dd_chart: Provide equity history to chart drawdown.
view.goals.h2.progress_chart: P/L progress vs window elapsed per goal
view.goals.h2.pace_chart: P/L pace distribution across all goals
view.goals.empty_pace_chart: No goal pace data to chart.
view.goals.tip.name: Human-readable label shown on the goal card and chart x-axis
view.goals.tip.period: Picks default start/end dates; 'custom' leaves them as you set them
view.goals.tip.account: Restrict progress to one account; (all) aggregates closed trades across accounts
view.goals.tip.start: First day counted in the window (inclusive, UTC)
view.goals.tip.end: Last day counted in the window (inclusive, UTC)
view.goals.tip.target_pnl: Dollar P/L target — pace chip flips green when projected end ≥ this
view.goals.tip.target_win: Win-rate target (0–100). Blank = no target, only show actual
view.goals.tip.max_dd: Max-drawdown cap (0–100). Going over flips the DD card red
view.goals.tip.create: Persist the goal; progress recomputes from closed trades in the window
view.goals.hint.no_goals_yet_create_one_: No goals yet — create one above.
view.greeks_profile.h2.normalized_chart: All greeks normalized to ±1 — relative shape comparison
view.greeks_profile.tip.kind: Call or Put — flips delta/rho sign + theta magnitude
view.greeks_profile.tip.strike: Contract strike; ATM marker sits at spot=strike on every chart
view.greeks_profile.tip.low: Lower bound of spot grid the greeks are evaluated across
view.greeks_profile.tip.points: Number of grid points (5–501); more = smoother curves
view.greeks_profile.tip.defaults: Snap low/high to ±50% of current strike
view.greeks_profile.tip.compute: Evaluate price + delta + gamma + vega + theta + rho across the grid
view.greeks_profile.hint.each_greek_plot:
Each greek plotted as a function of spot across the chosen grid. ATM marker
                shows where spot = strike. Quick sanity: ATM call delta ≈ 0.5, gamma peaks
                near ATM, vega peaks near ATM, theta most negative near ATM (max time-decay).
view.ha_reversal.h2.raw_close_ha_close_f: Raw close + HA close + flip markers
view.ha_reversal.h2.body_chart: Per-bar HA body ratio (|close − open| / range) — strong-flip threshold
view.ha_reversal.empty_body_chart: Provide bars to chart body ratio.
view.ha_reversal.hint.strong_flips_need_:
Strong flips need both a long prior same-color streak (default 3+)
                AND a decisive body (default ≥60% of bar range). Weak flips only need the
                shorter streak (default 2+) — earlier signal, less reliable.
view.ha_reversal.hint.magenta_raw_close_:
Magenta = raw close. Cyan = HA close. Green dot = BEAR→BULL
                flip (placed below HA low). Red dot = BULL→BEAR flip (placed above HA high).
view.halts.h2.category_chart: Halt count by category (news / volatility / circuit / regulatory / ipo / resume / other)
view.halts.empty_category_chart: No halts to chart by category.
view.heatmap.hint.dispersion: Low dispersion = sector moves in unison (macro/sector rotation). High dispersion = sector is being torn apart by single-name stories (idiosyncratic divergence).
view.heatmap.empty_dispersion_chart: No sector data to chart dispersion.
view.heatmap.hint.150_s_p_500_names_grou: ~150 S&P 500 names grouped by GICS sector, colored by today's % change. Your watchlist symbols add to a "Watchlist" pseudo-sector.
view.heatmap.hint.no_quotes_cached_yet_r: No quotes cached yet — refresh in a minute.
view.hotkeys.tip.name: Friendly label shown in the bindings table
view.hotkeys.tip.capture: Click then press any key combination; the next non-modifier keypress fills the combo field
view.hotkeys.tip.combo: Captured combo string (e.g. ctrl+shift+z); readonly — use Capture to set
view.hotkeys.tip.action: Action fired when this combo triggers; see ACTIONS list for the catalog
view.hotkeys.tip.save: Persist or update the binding (combo+action pair is unique)
view.hotkeys.tip.delete_row: Remove this binding; the combo becomes available again
view.hotkeys.hint.das_style_key_bindings: DAS-style key bindings. Click "capture" then press the desired combo.
view.hotkeys.th.name: Name
view.hurst.h2.cumulative_deviation_chart: Cumulative deviation from mean (R/S internal path)
view.hurst.hint.cumulative_deviation: Running sum of (return − mean). This is exactly the path whose range (max − min) becomes the R in R/S. Persistent series (H>0.5) wander far from zero; mean-reverting series (H<0.5) hug the yellow zero line.
view.hurst.empty_cumdev: Need at least 2 returns to chart cumulative deviation.
view.hurst.tip.returns: Return series (decimal); one per token; # comments OK; needs ≥ chunk_size×2
view.hurst.tip.chunks: Chunk sizes to evaluate R/S at; whitespace separated; smallest ≥ 8
view.hurst.tip.estimate: Compute R/S across chunks and regress log(n) vs log(R/S) for H
view.hurst.fit.clean: clean linear fit — H is trustworthy
view.hurst.hint.the_r_s_log_log_regressi:
The R/S log-log regression slope is the Hurst exponent. H < 0.5 =
                anti-persistent (mean-reverting), H ≈ 0.5 = random walk, H > 0.5 =
                persistent (long-memory / trending). R² flags fit quality — low R² means
                the series isn't well-described by a single Hurst exponent.
view.hurst.hint.cyan_log_n_vs_log_r_s_sc:
Cyan: log(n) vs log(R/S) scatter from the backend. Orange dashed: linear-
                fit line (slope = H). A clean straight line implies a meaningful Hurst
                estimate; scatter / curvature implies the series mixes regimes.
view.implementation_shortfall.hint.bps_c: Same components normalized as basis points of intended notional (qty × decision_mid). Order-size-invariant; lets you compare today's slice against any historical execution on a level field.
view.implementation_shortfall.empty_bps_: No cost components to chart in bps (need intended_qty × decision_mid > 0).
view.implementation_shortfall.hint.buy_c:
Buy convention: a positive $ cost means the trader paid up.
                Negative = captured liquidity / favorable drift. Total in bps
                normalizes against intended notional (qty × decision_mid).
view.import.hint.broker_chart: Cumulative row count per source across all uploads. Reveals which broker dominates your data lake and which sources have been silent.
view.import.empty_broker_chart: No imports to chart per broker.
view.import.tip.broker: Broker the CSV came from — picks the parser preset for column shapes
view.import.tip.dropzone: Drop a CSV here or click to pick one from disk
view.import.tip.upload: Upload the selected file; duplicates dedupe against existing executions
view.intraday_heatmap.empty_chart: No P&L data to chart per hour.
view.intraday_heatmap.hint.activity: When you actually trade vs where you make money. Opening drives, lunch lull, closing surge — overlay this against the P&L heatmap to see if your activity profile lines up with your profitability profile.
view.intraday_heatmap.empty_activity_cha: No trades to chart activity profile.
view.intraday_heatmap.tip.trades: One line per trade: 'timestamp pnl'; ISO 8601 or bare HH:MM
view.intraday_heatmap.tip.demo: Load 200 synthetic trades scattered across a trading day
view.intraday_heatmap.tip.build: Bucket trades into 15-min cells and render the heatmap + hourly chart
view.intraday_heatmap.tip.session: Show only 09:00-16:00 cells (cash-session); off shows the full 24h grid
view.intraday_heatmap.hint.color_intensi:
Color intensity scaled to the global max-abs PnL.
                Green = winning bucket. Red = losing. Dark = empty.
                Hover any cell for trade count / avg / win-rate.
view.iv_backtest.tip.implied: % the ATM straddle is pricing for the event (≈ straddle debit ÷ spot × 100)
view.iv_backtest.tip.backtest: Compare implied to historical realized; emit long/short verdict
view.iv_backtest.tip.realized: One % per line; sign kept for direction but P/L uses |realized|
view.iv_backtest.tip.demo: Load a 16-quarter realized>implied series — straddle was underpriced
view.iv_backtest.hint.implied_move_atm_s:
Implied move ≈ ATM straddle debit / spot × 100.
                If you're not sure, options chains usually print it as "Expected Move."
view.iv_backtest.hint.one_signed_value_p:
One signed value per line — the % move from the close before
                the event to the close after. Signs are kept for direction context but the
                backend computes on |realized|, since long-straddle P&L is symmetric.
view.iv_backtest.hint.bars_histogram_of_:
Bars = histogram of |realized| %. Orange dashed = implied move.
                If most of the mass sits to the RIGHT of the dashed line, realized has
                historically beaten implied — the straddle was underpriced.
view.iv_backtest.hint.each_dot_one_histo:
Each dot = one historical event's long-straddle P&L per $1 of premium.
                Positive = realized beat implied. Negative = implied was rich.
view.iv_rank.hint.vol_of_vol: Rolling 20-day stddev of the IV series. High = IV itself is moving fast (regime shift; earnings approach; vol crush risk). Low = IV is steady and rangebound. Yellow dashed = median across the window.
view.iv_rank.empty_vov_chart: Need at least 21 IV observations to chart vol-of-vol.
view.iv_rank.btn.load_demo_252_days_w_ea: Load demo (252 days w/ earnings spike)
view.iv_rank.tip.compute: Compute IV rank and percentile against pasted history
view.iv_rank.tip.history: One IV per line (decimal); whitespace + commas OK; # comments skipped
view.iv_rank.tip.demo: Load a synthetic 252-day series with an earnings spike — shows rank≫pctile
view.iv_rank.hint.paste_one_iv_value_per:
Paste one IV value per line (decimal). Whitespace, commas
                and # comments are tolerated.
view.iv_rank.hint.cyan_iv_series_orange_:
Cyan = IV series. Orange dashed = current IV. Green = 52w low.
                Red = 52w high. Magenta = IV percentile reference (current IV).
view.iv_solver.h2.vega_chart: Numerical vega vs σ (slope of price curve)
view.iv_solver.hint.vega: First difference of the BS price curve at each σ — that's vega (premium sensitivity per σ-point). Peaks near ATM σ; falls off at extremes. Green dashed = solved σ. The slope of the price curve at solved σ = your live vega.
view.iv_solver.empty_vega_chart: Not enough σ samples to derive vega.
view.iv_solver.tip.kind: Call or Put — flips intrinsic-value and the no-arb band
view.iv_solver.tip.mkt: Observed market premium; the solver rejects values outside the no-arb band
view.iv_solver.tip.solve: Newton-Raphson on BS until BS(σ) matches the market price
view.iv_solver.hint.newton_raphson_on_bl:
Newton-Raphson on Black-Scholes: finds σ such that BS(σ) matches the
                observed market price. Pre-flight rejects market prices outside the no-arb
                band — no IV exists there, would be a free arb if real.
view.iv_solver.hint.cyan_bs_price_curve_:
Cyan: BS price curve as σ sweeps from near-zero. Orange dashed line: your
                observed market price. Green marker: the solved σ — should sit exactly at
                the intersection. If the curve never crosses the line, no IV exists
                (out of no-arb bounds).
view.journal.hint.mood_dist: Frequency of each mood level across all entries. Reveals overall sentiment shape — heavy-tailed toward frustrated vs. centered on focused — independent of when each entry happened.
view.journal.empty_dist_chart: No mood-tagged entries to chart distribution.
view.journal.tip.day: Jump to a different day's journal — entries are scoped to this UTC date
view.journal.tip.mood: Optional mood score; appears next to the entry timestamp and feeds the trend chart
view.journal.tip.body: Markdown body; entries support inline code, lists, links
view.journal.tip.save: Persist the entry to this day (or to General if no date is bound)
view.journal.tip.insert_template: Append your default 'journal' note template to the body
view.journal_ai.hint.depth: Sum of characters across each category's items. Reveals depth vs. breadth: a category with 2 long detailed items may matter more than 4 one-word items. Compare against the count chart above.
view.journal_ai.empty_depth_chart: No findings to chart depth yet.
view.journal_ai.tip.provider: AI vendor — Anthropic/OpenAI need an API key; Ollama runs locally with none
view.journal_ai.tip.model: Model id passed to the provider; mismatched ids 4xx at request time
view.journal_ai.tip.endpoint: Override the provider default (e.g. self-hosted gateway, Ollama port)
view.journal_ai.tip.api_key: Stored encrypted; blank = keep current; redacted on read
view.journal_ai.tip.max_tokens: Cap on response tokens; lower = cheaper but may truncate JSON
view.journal_ai.tip.save: Persist settings; the next Run analysis uses these values
view.journal_ai.tip.run_analysis: Send the trade + journal to the LLM and cache the structured findings
view.journal_ai.hint.configure_a_provide:
Configure a provider for AI trade-journal analysis.
            Anthropic / OpenAI require an API key; Ollama runs locally with no key.
            The stored key is redacted on read — leave the field blank to keep the
            current key when saving other fields.
view.kalman_beta.tip.asset: Asset return series (y); one value per token; must match bench length
view.kalman_beta.tip.bench: Benchmark return series (x); the OLS independent leg
view.kalman_beta.tip.q: Process noise — higher = β adapts faster; lower = smoother trace
view.kalman_beta.tip.r: Observation noise — lower = trust returns more (β tracks noise)
view.kalman_beta.tip.beta0: Prior mean for β at t=0
view.kalman_beta.tip.p0: Prior variance for β at t=0; higher = quicker initial adaptation
view.kalman_beta.tip.run: Run the Kalman filter and overlay β trace + return series
view.kalman_beta.hint.lower_q_smoother_a:
Lower Q → smoother β (assumes slow drift). Higher Q → β adapts faster.
                Lower R → trust observations more (β tracks noise). Try Q=1e-4 + R=1e-4
                for daily equity pairs as a starting point.
view.kalman_beta.hint.cyan_per_bar_estim:
Cyan: per-bar β estimate from the Kalman filter. Orange dashed: β = 1
                reference (would mean perfect 1:1 hedge ratio).
view.kelly.hint.cum_pnl: Running sum of trade PnLs. Overlay against the rolling-Kelly chart above to see whether your equity grew during the trades that Kelly said to bet aggressively.
view.kelly.empty_cum_chart: No PnLs to chart cumulative equity.
view.kelly.tip.payoff: Average winning trade ÷ average losing trade; must be > 0
view.kelly.tip.run_static: Compute the optimal Kelly fraction from win-rate + payoff
view.kelly.tip.window: Lookback window (last N trades) for the rolling Kelly fraction
view.kelly.tip.run_dyn: Compute rolling Kelly across the trade-PnL history
view.kelly.tip.derive_static: Re-fill win-rate + payoff from the PnL textarea, then compute static
view.kelly.btn.derive_static_wr_payoff_f: Derive static (wr / payoff) from these PnLs
view.kelly.hint.cyan_raw_kelly_fraction_: Cyan = raw Kelly fraction. Yellow = half-Kelly (clamped ≥ 0). Red dashed = zero line.
view.liquidity.h2.pandl_and_win_rate_by_: P&L & win rate by ADV bucket
view.liquidity.empty_chart: No symbols with ADV matches to chart.
view.liquidity.h2.bucket_avg_pnl_chart: Avg P&L per trade by ADV bucket
view.liquidity.hint.bucket_avg_pnl: Per-bucket avg P&L per trade (net P&L / trades). Normalizes for bucket trade-count differences — reveals whether each bucket is profitable per-trade, not just in aggregate.
view.liquidity.empty_bucket_chart: No buckets with trades to chart avg P&L.
view.liquidity.tip.trades: Trade ledger: 'symbol qty net_pnl' per line; comments OK
view.liquidity.tip.adv: ADV table: 'symbol adv_shares' per line; symbols missing from ADV are dropped
view.liquidity.tip.demo: Load 4-symbol / 53-trade demo with a visible pnl cliff past 5% ADV
view.liquidity.tip.clear: Clear both trade and ADV textareas
view.liquidity.tip.analyze: Bucket by ADV participation; per-symbol + per-bucket breakdown
view.liquidity.hint.symbols_not_in_the_a:
Symbols not in the ADV table are silently dropped from
                bucket analysis. Per-symbol rows still show their qty / pnl regardless.
view.liquidity.hint.each_row_symbol_trad:
Each row: symbol · trade count · total qty · avg qty/trade ·
                ADV · avg % of ADV · liquidity tier · net pnl. Tiers cut at 0.1% / 1% / 5% / 20%.
view.liquidity.hint.if_your_wins_concent:
If your wins concentrate in small-pct buckets and losses
                concentrate in large-pct buckets, you have a sizing problem — not an edge problem.
view.live_positions.hint.snapshot_of_eve:
Snapshot of every open trade with fresh Yahoo quotes (60s
            server-cached). Unrealized P/L honors multiplier and side (long/short). Day P/L
            uses the quote's prev_close. Refreshes every 30 seconds.
view.live_scanner.empty_chart: No live ticks to chart yet.
view.live_scanner.hint.volume_chart: Sorted day volume across the live universe. Compare against change% above — a name moving big without volume is weaker than the chart suggests.
view.live_scanner.empty_volume_chart: No live volume to chart yet.
view.live_scanner.tip.voice: Toggle TTS announcements when symbols cross the +5% / -5% thresholds
view.live_scanner.tip.api_key: Finnhub key held in process memory only — never written to disk
view.live_scanner.tip.symbols: Comma-separated tickers; chunked across parallel WS connections as needed
view.live_scanner.tip.connect: Subscribe the universe to the live tick feed
view.live_scanner.hint.finnhub_s_free_we:
Finnhub's free WebSocket gives 25 subscriptions per connection.
                Larger universes chunk across parallel connections automatically.
                Key is held in process memory only — never written to disk.
view.market_impact.hint.scatter: Each trade as a point: qty/ADV on x, slippage bps on y. Continuous view of the cliff shape. Red dashed = spike threshold; trades above are paying the impact tax.
view.market_impact.empty_scatter_chart: No trades with positive ADV to scatter.
view.market_impact.tip.trades: Trade ledger: 'qty adv slippage_bps' per line; negative = favorable fill; ≥5 trades
view.market_impact.tip.demo: Load 400 demo trades that visibly cliff past 1% ADV participation
view.market_impact.tip.spike: First ADV bucket whose avg slippage clears this is flagged as cliff
view.market_impact.tip.analyze: Bucket trades by participation rate; flag the first cliff bucket
view.market_impact.hint.the_first_adv_bu:
The first ADV bucket whose avg slippage clears the threshold
                is flagged as your "impact cliff" — size below it.
view.market_impact.hint.per_bucket_trade:
Per bucket: trade count · avg · median · max slippage.
                Threshold-busting buckets glow red.
view.market_impact.hint.where_your_trade:
Where your trades actually land. If you have heavy mass past
                1% ADV you're paying the impact tax even if the average looks fine.
view.market_profile.h2.brackets_one_per_: Brackets (one per time period — typically 30-min RTH brackets)
view.market_profile.hint.cum_tpo: Cumulative share of TPOs walking from low to high. Yellow dashed at 15% / 85% = VAL / VAH reference; the steep middle = value-area density. Flat tails = single-print excess.
view.market_profile.empty_cum_chart: No TPO levels to chart cumulative %.
view.market_profile.hint.yellow_row_poc_:
Yellow row = POC (most time). Cyan rows = Value Area (70%).
                Red rows = single prints (excess — often retested). Letters are the
                actual TPO brackets that touched each price level.
view.mentorship.hint.scope_chart: Permission level across all connections. Complementary to the lifecycle status chart — reveals how much trust your mentors and mentees actually share.
view.mentorship.empty_scope_chart: No mentorship rows to chart scopes.
view.mentorship.h2.invite_a_mentor_paste: Invite a mentor (paste their user ID)
view.microprice.hint.bias: (microprice − midpoint) / midpoint × 10000 across the imbalance sweep. Positive = lean toward ask (next print likely upticks). Yellow dashed = zero. Orange marker = current snapshot.
view.microprice.empty_bias_chart: Need a valid bid/ask to chart bias.
view.microprice.tip.ask: Top-of-book ask price; must be ≥ bid
view.microprice.tip.bid_sz: Resting size at the bid; biases microprice toward ask when larger
view.microprice.tip.ask_sz: Resting size at the ask; biases microprice toward bid when larger
view.microprice.tip.compute: Send the snapshot to the Stoikov microprice endpoint
view.microprice.hint.microprice_bid_ask_:
Microprice = bid · (ask_size / total) + ask · (bid_size / total). When the bid
                queue dwarfs the ask queue, the next print is likely to lift the offer →
                microprice biases toward the ask.
view.microprice.hint.cyan_line_micropric:
Cyan line: microprice across every possible imbalance, given the current
                bid/ask spread. Orange marker: your current snapshot. The line interpolates
                linearly from bid (pure-ask-size queue, imbalance=0) to ask (pure-bid-size,
                imbalance=1).
view.monte_carlo.tip.model: Stochastic process; switching reloads default parameters
view.monte_carlo.tip.run: Execute the simulator and render terminal-distribution stats
view.monte_carlo.hint.density_curve_is_a:
Density curve is a normal approximation around (mean, stdev). For jump
                models the real distribution is skewed and fat-tailed — use the reported
                skew to gauge how misleading the normal is.
view.mood_analytics.hint.win_rate: Per-mood win rate (frequency view, not magnitude). Compare against avg P/L bars to see whether mood drives win frequency, win size, or both.
view.mood_analytics.empty_wr_chart: No mood-bucket stats to chart win rate.
view.mood_analytics.hint.no_mood_tagged_: No mood-tagged journal entries yet — tag your journal entries with a mood -2..+2 to seed this view.
view.mood_analytics.hint.no_r_multiples_: No R-multiples available (need risk_amount set on trades).
view.murrey_math.h2.distance_chart: Signed distance from current price to each Murrey level
view.murrey_math.hint.distance: Positive = level above current price; negative = level below. Yellow dashed = zero (current price). Reveals which levels are nearest and how clustered the S/R rail is.
view.murrey_math.empty_distance_chart: No Murrey levels to chart distance.
view.murrey_math.hint.cyan_close_yellow_:
Cyan = close. Yellow solid = 4/8 (major S/R mid). Red dashed = 8/8
                ultimate resistance + green dashed = 0/8 ultimate support. Grey dashed = octave
                interior + thin extension levels above/below.
view.murrey_math.hint.all_13_levels_2_8_:
All 13 levels (−2/8 to 10/8) with significance + distance from price.
                The two bracketing levels (immediately above and below current price) are highlighted.
view.new_trade.empty_qty_chart: No execution qty data to chart yet.
view.new_trade.tip.symbol: Ticker; uppercased on submit. / focuses this input
view.new_trade.tip.side: buy/sell open or close long; short/cover open or close short
view.new_trade.tip.qty: Share count for stocks; contract count for options/futures
view.new_trade.tip.price: Per-share fill price; per-contract premium for options
view.new_trade.tip.fee: Total commission + fees deducted from realized P/L
view.new_trade.tip.executed_at: Fill timestamp (local time); FIFO-folded by chronological order
view.new_trade.tip.asset_class: Asset type; option/future reveals strike/exp/multiplier fields
view.new_trade.tip.stop_loss: Optional stop price for Risk-Gate evaluation; not persisted on the execution
view.new_trade.tip.plan: Set if a trade-plan was attached pre-entry; gates plan-required rules
view.new_trade.tip.add: Submit; Risk Gate runs first (blocks fail loudly, warnings prompt)
view.new_trade.hint.add_executions_one_a: Add executions one at a time. The server FIFO-folds each one into the matching open trade (or starts a new one).
view.new_trade.hint.no_executions_yet_on: No executions yet on this account.
view.news.hint.symbol_sentiment: Mean sentiment across all loaded items per symbol. Yellow dashed = neutral. Reveals which tickers have the tape leaning bullish vs bearish independent of item count.
view.news.empty_symbol_chart: No sentiment data to chart per symbol.
view.news.hint.yahoo_headlines_polled_pe:
Yahoo headlines polled per watchlist symbol every 5 minutes,
            scored with the same WSB-aware sentiment lexicon used for social feeds, and
            indexed for full-text search via Postgres tsvector / websearch_to_tsquery.
            Color stripe on each row maps to sentiment: red (negative) → grey → green (positive).
view.news_event.hint.trim_qty: Absolute share count to trim per symbol. Complements the % chart — a 100% trim of 10 shares is less risk-removal than 50% trim of 1000 shares.
view.news_event.empty_qty_chart: No trim actions with positive quantity to chart.
view.news_event.btn.load_demo_5_position: Load demo (5 positions, 4 events spanning all tiers)
view.news_event.hint.trim_percent_by_imp:
Trim percent by impact: Low=0% (no action) · Medium=25% · High=50% · Critical=100% (full close).
                If multiple events affect a position, the highest-impact one wins.
view.oi_change.h2.pct_chart: Call vs put OI % change by strike
view.oi_change.h2.abs_chart: Call vs put Δ OI (absolute contracts) by strike
view.oi_change.hint.abs_chart: Absolute contract change per strike. A 1000% spike on a 50-OI strike is noise; a 30% increase on a 100k-OI strike is massive. This view filters % illusions.
view.oi_change.empty_abs_chart: No OI alerts to chart absolute changes.
view.oi_change.hint.sorted_biggest_absol:
Sorted biggest absolute change first. SURGE/STRONG rows
                are where upside positioning is concentrating today.

Sorted biggest absolute change first. SURGE on puts =
                institutional hedging or directional bearish bets.
view.open_type.h2.distance_chart: Signed distance from open price to each reference
view.open_type.hint.distance: Positive = level above open; negative = level below. Yellow dashed = zero (open). Reveals which references are closest and how the open sits in context (above VAH? below prior low?).
view.open_type.empty_distance_chart: Need a valid open price + at least one reference level.
view.open_type.hint.vah_val_the_70_volum:
VAH/VAL = the 70% volume-weighted value area from the prior session.
                Most market-profile platforms (Sierra, Bookmap) print these as horizontal lines
                on yesterday's profile.
view.open_type.hint.yellow_prior_h_l_ext:
Yellow = prior H/L (extreme range). Cyan = VAH/VAL (value area).
                Magenta = opening range H/L. Red dot = open price. Green dot = OR close.
                Distance + direction visually reveals the verdict.
view.optimal_f.hint.return_dist: Histogram of trade P/Ls feeding into the optimal-f computation. Reveals the edge shape — symmetric, skewed, fat-tailed, multimodal. Yellow dashed = zero (break-even).
view.optimal_f.tip.pnls: Trade P/L series (one signed value per token); + = win, − = loss; # comments OK
view.optimal_f.tip.compute: Run Vince's optimal-f solver; chart sweeps TWR(f) across 0–1
view.optimal_f.hint.optimal_f_the_fracti:
Optimal-f = the fraction of capital to risk per trade that maximizes
                long-run geometric growth (Vince 1990). Practitioners almost always use
                half- or quarter-Kelly because the TWR curve is asymmetric — overbetting
                by 50% loses more growth than underbetting by 50%.
view.optimal_f.hint.geometric_growth_cur:
Geometric growth curve from f = 0 to 1. Cyan: TWR. Vertical markers:
                optimal-f (orange), half-Kelly (cyan), quarter-Kelly (green). Notice the
                cliff to the right of optimal-f — overbetting wipes out wealth faster than
                underbetting forgoes it.
view.option_payoff.hint.delta: First difference of the at-expiry payoff at each spot — that's the strategy's terminal delta. Reveals where the position becomes net long, short, or flat across the spot range.
view.option_payoff.empty_delta_chart: Not enough spot samples to derive delta.
view.option_payoff.tip.preset: Preset overwrites the legs; switch to custom to keep edits
view.option_payoff.tip.spot: Current underlying price; recenters the payoff curve
view.option_payoff.tip.t: Time to expiry in years; affects the MTM dashed curve only
view.option_payoff.tip.rate: Risk-free rate (decimal) for the Black-Scholes MTM legs
view.option_payoff.tip.div_yield: Continuous dividend yield (decimal); enters the forward
view.option_payoff.tip.iv: Implied vol used for the MTM dashed curve (decimal)
view.option_payoff.tip.recalc: Re-run payoff + MTM and redraw the curve
view.option_payoff.tip.add_leg: Append a leg; defaults to a call at current spot
view.option_payoff.hint.solid_p_l_at_exp: Solid = P/L at expiry · Dashed = current MTM (Black-Scholes per leg)
view.options_chain.h2.iv_smile: IV smile (calls vs puts by strike)
view.options_chain.empty_chart: No IV data to chart for this expiration.
view.options_chain.h2.oi_chart: OI distribution by strike (calls vs puts)
view.options_chain.hint.oi_chart: Open interest per strike. Reveals magnet strikes for pin risk and institutional positioning independent of IV. Large peaks = where the smart money's already parked.
view.options_chain.empty_oi_chart: No OI data to chart for this expiration.
view.order_book_imbalance.h2.depth_chart: Bid vs ask size per level
view.order_book_imbalance.hint.per_level: Signed bid − ask at each level. Reveals if pressure is concentrated at L1 (HFT scalp signal) or spread across all levels (institutional commit). Yellow dashed = neutral.
view.order_book_imbalance.empty_imb_char: No levels to chart per-level imbalance.
view.order_book_imbalance.tip.bid: Bid sizes, level 1 first; one per line; # comments OK
view.order_book_imbalance.tip.ask: Ask sizes, level 1 first; one per line; need not match bid length
view.order_book_imbalance.tip.levels: Top-N levels to aggregate (1–50); deeper books smooth the imbalance
view.order_book_imbalance.tip.compute: Aggregate top-N levels and emit the [-1, 1] imbalance scalar
view.order_book_imbalance.hint.imbalance:
Imbalance = (Σbid − Σask) / (Σbid + Σask) over top-N levels.
                Range [-1, 1]. Used by HFT firms as a microsecond-scale directional signal.
view.order_book_imbalance.hint.cyan_bid_:
Cyan = bid skew. Magenta = ask skew. Midline = balanced.
                Quartile marks at ±0.1 (bid/ask) and ±0.3 (strong).
view.order_flow.parse.token_count: expected 4 tokens (price volume bid ask), got {n}
view.order_flow.hint.rolling_imb: Per-tick rolling (buy − sell) / (buy + sell) over a 20-tick window. Yellow dashed = neutral. Reveals when pressure shifts regime — accumulation cluster → distribution wave.
view.order_flow.empty_roll_chart: Need at least 21 ticks to chart rolling imbalance.
view.order_flow.tip.ticks: 'price volume bid ask' per line; ≥5 ticks; comments OK
view.order_flow.tip.demo: Load 400 engineered ticks with visible buy-side aggressor pressure
view.order_flow.tip.classify: Run Lee-Ready aggressor classification + roll up flow + imbalance
view.order_flow.hint.buy_sell_buy_sell_c:
(buy − sell) / (buy + sell). Cyan = aggressive buying,
                red = institutional dumping. Excludes uncertain volume.
view.order_flow.hint.cyan_cumulative_agg:
Cyan = cumulative aggressive-buy volume. Magenta (drawn
                negative for visual contrast) = cumulative sell. Yellow = net (buy − sell).
                Smooth-rising net = sustained accumulation. Steep drop = distribution.
view.order_staleness.hint.age_dist: Histogram of resting-order ages. Reveals the shape — long tail = forgotten cluster, peak near zero = active scalping cluster. Yellow dashed = your warn threshold.
view.order_staleness.empty_age_chart: No orders to chart age distribution.
view.order_staleness.btn.load_demo_12_or: Load demo (12 orders across all tiers)
view.order_staleness.tip.orders: Resting orders, one per line: id symbol placed_at [modified_at] side
view.order_staleness.tip.demo: Load 12 demo orders spanning fresh → forgotten tiers
view.order_staleness.tip.now: Reference time (ISO 8601) all age comparisons are measured against
view.order_staleness.tip.now_now: Set the reference time to the current wall-clock
view.order_staleness.tip.warn: Hours after placement to flag as WARN tier
view.order_staleness.tip.stale: Hours after placement to flag as STALE; cancel candidates
view.order_staleness.tip.forgotten: Hours after placement to flag as FORGOTTEN; almost certainly cancel
view.order_staleness.tip.evaluate: Bucket orders into fresh/warn/stale/forgotten relative to Now
view.order_staleness.hint.industry_defau:
Industry defaults: 24h warn / 72h stale / 168h forgotten.
                Tight intraday traders use 1h/4h/24h. Position traders use 7d/30d/90d.
view.order_staleness.hint.stale_forgotte:
STALE/FORGOTTEN rows are candidates to cancel. Most accidental
                "what was that fill?" P&L surprises come from forgotten resting orders.
view.pair_trade.tip.y: Dependent leg prices, one per line; # comments OK
view.pair_trade.tip.x: Independent (hedge) leg prices, one per line; must match y length
view.pair_trade.tip.entry: Enter trade when |z| exceeds this; convention: 2.0σ
view.pair_trade.tip.exit: Exit when |z| falls below this; must be < entry
view.pair_trade.tip.stop: Bail when |z| exceeds this — cointegration likely broken; must be > entry
view.pair_trade.tip.analyze: Fit OLS β, compute z, evaluate signal against the bands
view.pair_trade.hint.is_fit_by_ols_regre:
β is fit by OLS regression of y on x. Spread = y − β·x. Z = (spread − mean) /
                stdev. Convention: positive z → spread expensive (sell y, buy x); negative
                z → spread cheap (buy y, sell x). exit_z must be < entry_z; stop_z must be > entry_z.
view.pair_trade.hint.if_is_right_the_two:
If β is right, the two lines track each other and their difference (the
                spread) oscillates around 0. Visible drift between them = the spread is
                widening — opportunity if mean-reverting, danger if cointegration broke.
view.pair_trade.hint.cyan_z_score_dashed:
Cyan: z-score. Dashed orange: ±entry band. Dashed red: ±stop band. Bars
                outside the entry band would have triggered an entry signal; bars between
                ±exit band would have triggered an exit.
view.pairs.hint.pearson_correlation_matr:
Pearson correlation matrix over log-returns + per-pair OLS spread & z-score
        (mean-reversion stat-arb signal — |z| ≥ 2 → consider fading).
view.paper.empty_chart: No quotes available to chart unrealized P&L.
view.paper.hint.notional: Per-symbol capital allocation (qty × last). Reveals concentration risk independent of P/L — a 60% notional in one name is concentration even if it's green today.
view.paper.empty_notional_chart: No quotes available to chart notional.
view.paper.tip.symbol: Ticker to trade; uppercased on submit
view.paper.tip.side: BUY opens long; SELL closes long; SHORT opens short; COVER closes short
view.paper.tip.order_type: Market = fill at last; Limit = needs price ≤/≥ limit; Stop = trigger at price
view.paper.tip.limit: Limit price; required only when order type = limit
view.paper.tip.stop: Stop trigger price; required only when order type = stop
view.paper.tip.submit: Send the order to the paper account; fills immediately if marketable
view.paper.tip.reset: Wipe all positions + orders and refund the account to $200k starting cash
view.pattern_discovery.tip.m: Window length; pattern duration. Series must have ≥ 2·m samples
view.pattern_discovery.tip.top_k: Number of discord (anomaly) windows to highlight (1–20)
view.pattern_discovery.tip.discover: Compute the matrix profile and surface motifs + top discords
view.pattern_discovery.tip.series: One value per token; whitespace/comma/newline separated; # comments OK
view.pattern_discovery.hint.m_sets_the_p:
m sets the pattern length. Low matrix-profile values = repeated patterns (motifs);
                high values = anomalies (discords). Series must have ≥ 2·m samples.
view.pattern_discovery.hint.each_x_is_a_: Each x is a window start. Low = best match found elsewhere; high = no good match (anomaly).
view.per_symbol_slippage.hint.stdev: Per-symbol stdev of slippage bps. Reveals which names have wildly variable execution — a 0-mean / 30-stdev name is just as bad as a -10-mean / 5-stdev one. Stable execution names sit at the bottom.
view.per_symbol_slippage.empty_stdev_cha: No symbols to chart slippage stdev.
view.per_symbol_slippage.tip.records: 'symbol slippage_bps' per line; positive = trader-favorable; # comments OK
view.per_symbol_slippage.tip.demo: Load 108-fill / 6-symbol demo spanning ETF to micro-cap execution quality
view.per_symbol_slippage.tip.aggregate: Roll up per-symbol mean/stdev + execution grade; rank worst-first
view.per_symbol_slippage.hint.sorted_wor:
Sorted worst-mean-first. POOR / TERRIBLE rows are where
                your sizing is bigger than the book can absorb — shrink position there
                or stop trading the name.
view.plans.empty_chart: No plans with R:R to chart.
view.plans.h2.risk_chart: Stop distance per plan (sorted widest first)
view.plans.hint.risk_chart: |entry − stop| per share. Wide stops mean smaller position for a fixed risk budget; tight stops mean larger. Critical for sizing decisions across the plan set.
view.plans.empty_risk_chart: No plans with stop loss to chart risk.
view.portfolio_allocator.tip.cov: Symmetric N×N annualized covariance matrix; rows whitespace or comma separated
view.portfolio_allocator.tip.labels: Optional one-label-per-line; padded with positional names if missing
view.portfolio_allocator.tip.excess: Optional annualized excess returns for tangency; blank ⇒ flat 5%
view.portfolio_allocator.tip.allocate: Run min-variance, max-diversification, and ERC allocators in parallel
view.portfolio_allocator.hint.mv_solver_: MV solver returned null (covariance not invertible?).
view.position_size.empty_chart: Compute a sizing to chart per-method results.
view.position_size.hint.notional: Capital deployment view. Notional $ on cyan; % of equity (×1000 for axis-share) on purple. Reveals which method commits the most capital regardless of share count.
view.position_size.empty_notional_chart: No sized methods to chart notional.
view.position_size.hint.three_industry_m:
Three industry methods side-by-side. Risk-per-share is computed
            from the entry/stop distance per side. Correlation drag halves your share count
            at ρ=1 (one perfectly-correlated open position), reduces by 33% at ρ=0.5, and so on.
            Kelly defaults to half-Kelly because full Kelly is brutal in real drawdowns.
view.premarket.hint.atr: Overnight move as a multiple of the security's own 20-day ATR. ≥1.0× = statistically significant. Reveals true-significance moves that raw % hides (a 0.3% FX move at 3× ATR is bigger news than a 5% crypto move at 0.4× ATR).
view.premarket.empty_atr_chart: No premarket contracts with ATR-multiple data.
view.premarket.hint.cross_asset_overnigh:
Cross-asset overnight tape: index futures, commodities, crypto, FX.
            Each gap is normalized by 20-day ATR — magnitudes above 1.0× ATR are statistically
            significant moves vs the security's own recent volatility. High-importance economic
            releases scheduled for today appear at the bottom. Refreshes every 30s.
view.pyramid.h2.total_qty_chart: Total position size after each tranche
view.pyramid.hint.total_qty: Cumulative qty after each tranche fires. Pyramid-Up: tapering adds = decreasing slope (smaller-each-add discipline). Scale-In: equal/larger adds = steeper slope (martingale risk).
view.pyramid.empty_qty_chart: No tranche states to chart cumulative qty.
view.pyramid.th.label: Label
view.r_distribution.hint.tag_samples: Trade count per tag. Reveals which tags have meaningful sample sizes vs noisy low-sample tags. A 0.5R mean over 200 trades is real edge; over 3 trades it's noise.
view.r_distribution.empty_samples_chart: No tagged trades to chart sample counts.
view.r_distribution.hint.r_net_pnl_risk_:
R = net_pnl ÷ risk_amount per trade — requires risk_amount set
            on entry. Histogram bins 0.5R from -5R to +5R with tails clamped to the edges.
            SQN = √N × mean(R) ÷ stdev(R) per Van Tharp: under 1.6 poor, 1.6-1.9 below average,
            2.0-2.4 average, 2.5-2.9 good, 3.0-5.0 excellent, >5 suspect (likely curve-fit).
view.r_distribution.hint.no_tagged_trade: No tagged trades with risk_amount set.
view.range_expansion.h2.range_atr_chart: Per-bar range / ATR with compression and expansion thresholds
view.range_expansion.hint.range_atr: Bar range as a multiple of ATR. Yellow dashed = prior_atr_max (compression cap); green dashed = min_expansion (expansion floor). Sequences that dip below yellow then spike above green are the textbook setup.
view.range_expansion.empty_ratio_chart: Need bars + ATR to chart range/ATR ratio.
view.range_expansion.hint.industry_defau:
Industry defaults (Raschke): ATR-14, lookback 5, ≥1.5× ATR for
                the expansion bar, <0.7× ATR for at least one compression bar in the lookback.
                Prior-ATR-max must be < min-expansion-atrs (compression then expansion).
view.range_expansion.hint.cyan_close_yel:
Cyan = close. Yellow = ATR(period). Green dot above bar =
                UP expansion. Red dot below bar = DOWN expansion. Marker placement reveals
                direction at a glance.
view.rebalance.hint.trade_value: Signed dollar amount to trade per symbol. Positive = buy, negative = sell. Reveals absolute dollar magnitude of the rebalance independent of drift %.
view.rebalance.empty_trade_chart: No positions to chart trade values.
view.rebalance.tip.preset: Pick a canned weighting; selecting one overwrites the targets editor
view.rebalance.tip.cash: Idle cash to spread into the rebalance plan
view.rebalance.tip.max_trades: Hard cap on trade count — engine drops smallest-impact trades first
view.rebalance.tip.compute_plan: Snapshot positions, fetch quotes, and build the trade list
view.rebalance.tip.download_csv: Download the trade list as CSV for manual broker entry
view.rebalance.tip.targets: JSON array of {symbol, weight} pairs — weights are 0–1, not percentages
view.rebalance.hint.set_target_weights_p:
Set target weights per symbol; the engine snapshots your open
            positions, fetches fresh quotes, and computes the exact whole-share trade list
            to drift back to target. Max-trades cap keeps a rebalance from blowing into 30+
            orders. Export trades as CSV for manual broker entry — TraderView never sends orders.
view.regime_detector.hint.realized_vol: Rolling 20-bar mean of |return| — model-free realized vol. Compare against the P(state=1) chart above to see how realized vol shifts BEFORE the Markov filter classifies it.
view.regime_detector.empty_vol_chart: Need at least 21 returns to chart rolling realized vol.
view.regime_detector.tip.bpy: Bars per year for annualization (252 daily, 52 weekly, 12 monthly)
view.regime_detector.tip.detect: Fit Hamilton-Kim filter + Baum-Welch EM and classify bars
view.regime_detector.tip.returns: Daily returns (decimal); one per token; comments OK
view.regime_detector.hint.2_state_markov:
2-state Markov-switching via Hamilton-Kim filter + Baum-Welch EM. State 1 is
                the higher-vol state by convention. Annualization uses √N scaling for σ and
                linear scaling for μ.
view.regime_detector.hint.returns_on_the:
Returns on the left axis (grey). State-1 probability on the right axis (red);
                values near 1 mark bars classified as the high-vol regime.
view.regime_equity.validate.equity_min: need ≥ 3 equity points (linear fit requires at least 3)
view.regime_equity.validate.equity_finit: all equity values must be finite numbers
view.regime_equity.h2.paste_equity_curve: Paste equity curve (one value per line, or CSV / whitespace-separated)
view.regime_equity.hint.residual: Per-bar residual from the trend line. Yellow dashed = zero. Tight band around zero = trending; wide swings = volatile. Visualizes the residual variance that drives the regime classification.
view.regime_equity.empty_residual_chart: Need at least 2 equity values to chart residuals.
view.regime_equity.hint.cyan_your_equity: Cyan = your equity. Yellow = OLS regression fit. Spread of equity around the fit ⇒ residual stdev ⇒ trending vs volatile classification.
view.replay.tip.day: Jump to a different trading day's closed trades
view.replay.tip.trade_pick: Select a trade to replay; chart + execs rerender on change
view.replay.hint.cum_qty: Signed running qty (buys +, sells −) across executions in order. Reveals whether the trade was averaged in gradually, pyramid'd with smaller adds, or fired single-shot.
view.replay.empty_qty_chart: No executions to chart cumulative qty.
view.replay.hint.no_closed_trades_on_thi: No closed trades on this day.
view.reports.hint.r_multiple_net_p_l_ris: R-multiple = net P&L / risk amount. Populate stop_loss + risk_amount on each trade to get these numbers.
view.reports.hint.annualized_values_assu: Annualized values assume 252 trading days/year and rf = 0.
view.research.h3.eps_estvactual_chart: EPS estimate vs actual per period
view.research.hint.eps_estvactual: Estimate vs actual side-by-side. Persistent positive surprises = analyst sandbagging; persistent negatives = optimistic estimates.
view.research.empty_estact_chart: No EPS estimate/actual pairs to chart.
view.research.tip.symbol: Anything Yahoo recognizes — stocks, ^indices, =F futures, -USD crypto
view.research.tip.submit: Load the per-symbol research page; r in detail view refreshes
shortcut.research_action: Submit search; in detail mode refreshes the page
view.research.hint.tip_anything_yahoo_re: Tip: anything Yahoo recognizes works — stocks, indices (^FTSE), futures (CL=F), crypto (BTC-USD).
view.risk.tip.today_pnl: Net P/L for trades closed today (UTC date)
view.risk.tip.daily_goal: Profit target; goal-progress bar fills as Today P/L approaches this
view.risk.tip.max_loss: Loss cap; max-loss bar fills as Today P/L approaches -this
view.risk.tip.alltime_net: Cumulative net P/L across every closed trade on this account
view.risk.tip.goal_input: Daily profit goal — 0 disables the goal bar
view.risk.tip.max_input: Daily max loss (positive number) — 0 disables the loss tracker
view.risk.tip.save: Persist the limits; both bars re-render from the new values
view.risk.hint.set_a_daily_profit_goal_u: Set a daily profit goal under Settings to enable progress tracking.
view.risk.hint.set_a_daily_max_loss_unde: Set a daily max loss under Settings to enable the tracker.
view.risk_gate.intro: Pre-trade rules that veto bad trades before they reach the broker. discipline tells you what you already broke; this stops the next one. Engine: traderview_core::risk_gate (pure-compute, 18 unit tests).
view.vol_smile.intro: Paste quotes below — one per line, two whitespace-OR-comma separated fields: strike iv. IV accepts 0.25 or 25%. Lines starting with # are skipped.
view.tax_workshop.intro: Trader-as-business calculators. Math lives in traderview-expense and is unit-tested against hand-rolled IRS scenarios. Inputs are local-only — nothing leaves your machine in desktop mode.
view.halts.intro: Polling nasdaqtrader.com/rss.aspx?feed=tradehalts every 3 seconds. New halts speak the symbol + reason via Web Speech API.
view.tax_lots.hint.skipped_short: {n} short/cover or oversold event(s) were skipped (short-side lot tracking is a v2 feature).
view.tutorial.intro: Press <kbd>?</kbd> any time to re-open this. Press <kbd>Cmd</kbd>+<kbd>K</kbd> for the launcher.
view.risk_gate.btn.beginner_strict_1_tra: Beginner (strict — 1% trade, 3% day, requires plan + stop)
view.risk_gate.hint.live_ping_of_the_gat: Live ping of the gate with a near-zero-risk synthetic trade — shows which rules would fire on a probe entry RIGHT NOW. Click refresh after every trade close.
view.risk_gate.hint.one_click_curated_ru: One-click curated rule pack. Existing rules are kept — review + delete after install if you want a clean slate.
view.risk_gate.hint.which_rules_trigger_: Which rules trigger most. High-fire rules are working hard; zero-fire rules might be too lenient.
view.rr_butterfly.hint.fx_vol_quotes_are:
FX vol quotes are conventionally in vol-points (percent). RR > 0 = calls
                priced richer than puts (right tail fear); BF > 0 = wings priced richer
                than ATM (smile curvature premium).
view.scanners.hint.warrior_zendoo_style_:
Warrior/Zendoo-style preset scans across your watchlist universe.
        Click a preset to run.
view.screener.tip.watchlist: Symbol universe to scan; blank = union of every watchlist you own
view.screener.tip.min_score: Lower bound on composite signal score (-10..+10); blank = no floor
view.screener.tip.max_score: Upper bound on composite signal score; blank = no cap
view.screener.tip.summary: Filter hits by overall verdict; (any) keeps the full result set
view.screener.tip.days: Lookback used for RSI/SMA/MACD inputs — must be ≥ longest period
view.screener.tip.limit: Hard cap on returned rows; ranks by score descending
view.screener.tip.run: Compute signals across the universe and rank by score
view.screener.hint.runs_technical_signal: Runs technical signals across your watchlists, returns ranked hits. Score range -10..+10.
view.search.empty_rank_chart: No journal/forum rank data to chart.
view.search.tip.q: Free-text query — symbol, journal body, forum title/body; / focuses this field
view.search.tip.scope: Restrict the search to one corpus; 'all' searches trades + journal + forum
view.search.tip.search: Run the full-text search and rank hits across scopes
view.second_order_greeks.hint.grid_value:
Grid values computed client-side (BS closed-form). The "backend ATM"
                card calls /options/calc/second-order-greeks once at spot = strike for a
                canonical reference — local + backend should agree to ~7 dp.
view.sector_rotation.empty_chart: No sector rotation data to chart.
view.sectors.hint.11_spdr_sector_etfs_ra: 11 SPDR sector ETFs ranked by today's % change and relative strength vs SPY (ZenBot-style).
view.sentiment.hint.lexicon_scorer_wsb_a:
Lexicon scorer (WSB-aware) over Reddit r/wallstreetbets new posts +
            StockTwits symbol streams. Auto-polls every 60s server-side; this
            view also refreshes every 60s.
view.sentiment.hint.no_symbols_match_in_: No symbols match in this window yet.
view.sentiment.hint.no_posts_cached_yet_: No posts cached yet — try "Poll now".
view.sentiment.th.author: Author
view.series_smoother.tip.series: One value per token; whitespace/comma/newline separated; # comments OK
view.series_smoother.tip.smooth: Run every ticked smoother in parallel and overlay them on the chart
view.settings.label.require_account_tag: Always require account tag on import
view.settings.hint.crt_scanlines_neon_bo: CRT scanlines, neon-border pulse, and dark/light theme are toggled from the buttons in the topbar. Color scheme switches the whole HUD palette — picks below.
view.settings.hint.commission_rates_fill:
Commission rates fill in only when the broker file omits fees (fee = 0).
                Mirrors TraderVue's "manual rate" behavior — won't double-count.
view.settings.hint.share_this_with_someo: Share this with someone if they want to mentor you.
view.settings.th.name: Name
view.setups_by_setup.empty_wlr_chart: No tagged trades to chart wins/losses.
view.setups_by_setup.hint.risk_amount_en: Risk amount enables R-multiple. Net P&L > 0 = win, < 0 = loss, == 0 = scratch. Untagged trades ("-") are sent to the backend but excluded from any setup bucket — matches backend "no setup tag → not in the catalog".
view.short_interest.h2.watchlist_ranking: Watchlist ranking (sorted by short % of float)
view.short_interest.h2.finra_reg_sho_dai: FINRA Reg SHO daily short volume (last 30 sessions)
view.short_interest.hint.yahoo_defaultke:
Yahoo defaultKeyStatistics for shares short / float % / days-to-cover,
            FINRA Reg SHO daily short-volume aggregated across market centers.
view.short_interest.hint.no_finra_data_f: No FINRA data — file may be embargoed or pending.
view.signal_decomposition.hint.each_subp:
Each subplot is one component. For EMD: high-freq IMFs first, residual last.
                For Wavelet: detail levels first, smooth approximation last. For SSA: trend
                on top, noise residual below.
view.spread_tracker.hint.regime_cuts_at_:
Regime cuts at 5 / 25 / 100 bps. Wide → use limit orders.
                Pathological → feed broken or illiquid name; sit out.
view.spread_tracker.hint.cyan_spread_bps:
Cyan = spread bps. Dashed reference lines: green = 5 bps (tight),
                yellow = 25 bps (normal cap), red = 100 bps (pathological cliff).
view.spread_tracker.hint.just_so_you_see:
Just so you see if the spread blew out alongside a real
                price move (newsy event) vs. a pure feed glitch (mid steady).
view.squeeze_alerts.empty_sym_chart: No per-symbol event data to chart.
view.squeeze_alerts.btn.load_demo_3_symb: Load demo (3 symbols, 60 ticks each, 30 min span)
view.squeeze_alerts.hint.browser_autopla:
Browser autoplay policy: audio context unlocks after your first
                click anywhere on the page. If "Test bell" fails silently, click any button on
                the page first then retry.
view.squeeze_alerts.hint.all_settings_pe:
All settings persist in localStorage. Watchlist gates which
                symbols can fire — leave blank to alert on every symbol that breaches.
view.stop_loss_best_of.hint.9_candidates:
9 candidates by default: None / $1 / $2 / 0.5% / 1% / 2% / 1×ATR / 2×ATR / 3×ATR.
                Each is simulated against all trades; results ranked by total realized P&L.
view.strategy_alerts.empty_truth_chart: No rules to chart truth distribution.
view.strategy_alerts.tip.name: Human-readable rule name shown in the alerts table
view.strategy_alerts.tip.template: Pick a canned AST shape; selecting one overwrites the JSON editor
view.strategy_alerts.tip.create: Persist the rule and start evaluating it on the 60s server tick
view.strategy_alerts.tip.ast: Rule AST as JSON — leaf or and/or/not over price/RSI/SMA/EMA/quote
view.strategy_alerts.tip.evaluate_now: Re-evaluate every rule immediately instead of waiting for the next 60s tick
view.strategy_alerts.hint.compound_and_o:
Compound AND/OR/NOT rules over price + RSI/SMA/EMA + change-pct +
            pct-of-high + ^VIX / ^CPC / ^ADD quote leaves. Server evaluates every 60s and fires
            on the false→true edge so a persistent condition won't spam. Connected webhooks
            fan-out on every fire. AST is JSON; pick a template to start.
view.strategy_alerts.th.name: Name
view.stress_test.h2.curve_chart: P&L vs price shock (per IV shock)
view.stress_test.empty_chart: Run a stress test to chart the curves.
view.stress_test.hint.negative_price_sho:
Negative price-shock = downside; negative IV-shock = vol crush.
                Worst-case + best-case cells highlighted in the heatmap below.
view.stress_test.hint.each_cell_portfoli:
Each cell = portfolio P&L under that shock pair (with time-decay
                applied). Gold border = worst-case cell. Cyan border = best-case cell.
                Hover any cell for full greeks under that shock.
view.tape.hint.auto_refreshing_news_sect: Auto-refreshing news + sector snapshot from your watchlist universe. Updates every 30 seconds.
view.tape.hint.no_news_in_this_universe_: No news in this universe yet.
view.tape.hint.add_symbols_to_a_watchlis: Add symbols to a watchlist first.
view.tape_replay.empty_range_chart: No bar range data to chart.
view.tape_replay.empty_chart: No bar volume data to chart.
view.tape_replay.h2.what_i_d_do_differen: What I'd do differently (client-side, this session only)
view.tape_replay.hint.re_watch_the_exact:
Re-watch the exact bar sequence around any of your closed trades
            with execution markers fading in at their fill timestamps. Auto-picks bar interval
            from hold duration (1m/5m/1h/1d). Use the replay to ask "what would I do differently"
            and drop client-side journal notes at the cursor.
view.tape_replay.hint.no_closed_trades_o: No closed trades on this account.
view.tape_replay.hint.no_bars_cached_for: No bars cached for this trade window.
view.tax_lots.hint.no_account_selected_c: No account selected. Create one on the Accounts tab first.
view.tax_lots.hint.pick_a_year_method_an: Pick a year + method and run.
view.tax_lots.hint.no_closed_lots_in_thi: No closed lots in this year.
view.tax_lots.hint.holding_period_clock_:
Holding-period clock on open lots is current as of now — closing
                these positions today would realize at their displayed term.
view.tax_workshop.empty_subs_chart: Run the subscription detector to populate the chart.
view.tax_workshop.empty_chart: Run the quarterly forecast to populate the chart.
view.tax_workshop.hint.add_trips_irs_sta: Add trips. IRS standard rate is applied per trip date (handles 2022 mid-year split).
view.tax_workshop.hint.scans_your_import: Scans your imported transactions for monthly/quarterly/annual charges with stable amounts. Largest annual leak first.
view.three_bar_reversal.btn.load_demo_14: Load demo (14 bars, bull + bear pattern)
view.three_bar_reversal.hint.cyan_close_:
Cyan = close. Green dot = bullish reversal (placed below the
                third bar's low). Red dot = bearish reversal (placed above the third bar's high).
view.time_in_force.h2.age_chart: Order age (days since placed) vs cutoffs
view.top_signals.tip.watchlist: Symbol universe to score; blank unions every watchlist you own
view.top_signals.tip.limit: Max rows per side; ranks by score descending
view.top_signals.tip.refresh: Re-score the universe and rebuild both BUY and SELL lists
view.top_signals.hint.highest_and_lowest: Highest- and lowest-scoring symbols across your watchlists (StockInvest-style ranking).
view.trade_compare.h2.normalized_p_l_ove: Normalized P/L overlay (% return vs entry)
view.trade_detail.h2.excursion_chart: MFE vs MAE vs Net P&L
view.trade_reviews.hint.inbox_zero_every: ✓ Inbox zero — every high-|R| trade has been reviewed.
view.trades.tip.refresh: Re-fetch the trade list from the server
view.trades.tip.rollup: Re-run the FIFO trade-formation over every execution in this account
view.trades.tip.close_exp: Mark expired option trades as closed at $0; idempotent
view.trades.tip.bulk_action: Action applied to every checked row when Apply is clicked
view.trades.tip.apply: Run the bulk action against the selected rows
view.triple_screen.hint.in_an_up_tide_pu:
In an UP tide, pull-back below oversold = entry zone.
                In a DOWN tide, rally above overbought = entry zone.
view.tutorial.hint.always_on_streaming_t: Always-on streaming tiles. Each opens its own WebSocket; voice alerts use the browser SpeechSynthesis API.
view.tutorial.hint.most_feeds_are_free_p: Most feeds are free public endpoints — no auth required:
view.twap.validate.side: side must be long or short
view.twap.h2.typical_prices_over_the_exp: Typical prices over the exposure window
view.twap.h2.typical_price_rolling_twap_: Typical price + rolling TWAP + fill reference
view.twap.btn.load_demo_200_prices_fill_: Load demo (200 prices, fill beats TWAP)
view.twap.hint.one_value_per_line_typica:
One value per line. Typical = (H+L+C)/3 from each bar.
                Demo loads 200 typicals with a long fill engineered to beat the mean.
view.twap.hint.cyan_typical_yellow_rolli:
Cyan = typical. Yellow = rolling TWAP (arithmetic mean to bar i).
                Magenta dashed = fill. Long entries want magenta below yellow; shorts the inverse.
view.var_calculator.tip.lambda: EWMA decay (0.5–0.999); 0.94 is RiskMetrics default, ~25-day half-life
view.var_calculator.tip.compute: Run Historical, Filtered-Historical, and Cornish-Fisher VaR in parallel
view.var_calculator.tip.returns: Daily returns (decimal); one per line or comma/space separated, # for comments
view.var_calculator.hint.confidence_0_95:
Confidence 0.95 = 95% VaR (5% tail). Higher confidence ⇒ deeper-in-tail estimate.
                EWMA λ controls how quickly FHS adapts to recent vol — 0.94 is the RiskMetrics
                default (≈ 25-day half-life).
view.var_calculator.hint.histogram_of_pa:
Histogram of pasted returns. Vertical lines mark the negative of each
                method's VaR (a 5% VaR of 2% appears as a line at −0.02 on the x-axis).
view.var_estimator.hint.historical_empir: Historical = empirical percentile of loss distribution. Parametric assumes Gaussian (μ ± z·σ). When historical > parametric, fat tails are punishing your model. Expected Shortfall (CVaR) = mean loss BEYOND VaR — always ≥ VaR.
view.var_estimator.hint.histogram_of_dai: Histogram of daily losses. Yellow dashed = historical VaR, red dashed = historical ES, cyan dashed = parametric VaR.
view.vasicek.tip.r0: Initial short-rate (decimal); the simulation starts here at t=0
view.vasicek.tip.a: Mean-reversion speed; half-life = ln(2)/a in years
view.vasicek.tip.b: Long-run mean (decimal); the rate that r drifts toward
view.vasicek.tip.dt: Time-step in years (e.g. 1/52 for weekly, 1/252 for daily)
view.vasicek.tip.steps: Steps per path; total horizon = steps × dt years
view.vasicek.tip.paths: Monte Carlo paths; standard error shrinks as 1/√N
view.vasicek.tip.simulate: Run the OU simulation; report terminal stats + negative-path fraction
view.vasicek.hint.half_life_of_mean_reve:
Half-life of mean reversion = ln(2)/a. Default a=0.5 → half-life ≈ 1.39
                years. Vasicek allows negative rates — the report tells you what fraction
                of paths visited a negative value, which is real risk for European-rate
                modeling post-2014.
view.vasicek.hint.density_centered_on_th:
Density centered on the simulated (mean, stdev). Vasicek's terminal rate
                is asymptotically normal, so the curve is exact in the long-run limit and
                a good approximation for any reasonably-long horizon.
view.vol.empty_chart: No yield change data to chart.
view.vol_smile.tip.spot: Underlying spot price; used to compute log-moneyness for each strike
view.vol_smile.tip.t_years: Time to expiry in years (e.g. 30/365 for monthlies)
view.vol_smile.tip.rate: Risk-free rate (decimal); enters the forward / discount factor
view.vol_smile.tip.div_yield: Continuous dividend yield (decimal); reduces the forward
view.vol_smile.tip.fit: Solve SVI parameters and overlay the fitted curve
view.vol_smile.tip.quotes: One strike / IV per line, whitespace or comma separated; IV as 0.25 or 25%
view.vol_smile.hint.solid_svi_fitted_cur: Solid = SVI fitted curve · Markers = raw paste
view.vol_stop_close.hint.both_stops_use_: Both stops use the same ATR + multiplier. Close-based references highest CLOSE in window; Chandelier references highest HIGH. On the wick-spike demo, watch Chandelier ratchet up to the wick while close-based ignores it.
view.vol_stop_close.hint.cyan_close_yell: Cyan = close. Yellow = close-based vol-stop. Red dashed = chandelier (high-based). Red dots = triggers (filled = close-based, hollow = chandelier).
view.vol_surface.hint.enter_a_symbol_to_: Enter a symbol to fetch its surface.
view.vpin.tip.ticks: Tick stream: 'price volume' per line; # comments OK; ≥ ~10 ticks needed
view.vpin.tip.demo: Load 1500-tick demo: calm regime then a toxic burst crossing the 0.5 line
view.vpin.tip.vpb: Equal-volume bucket size; smaller = finer-grained VPIN time series
view.vpin.tip.wb: Rolling window of buckets used in each VPIN computation
view.vpin.tip.rw: Return window in ticks for BVC classification of buy vs sell volume
view.vpin.tip.compute: Run BVC + bucketed VPIN; render time series + buy/sell volume bars
shortcut.vpin_compute: Compute VPIN from the tick stream
view.vpin.hint.cyan_buy_classified_volum: Cyan = buy-classified volume. Magenta = sell-classified. Vertical asymmetry per bucket = imbalance = VPIN's numerator.
view.vwap_slippage.validate.side: side must be long or short
view.vwap_slippage.h2.bars_during_the_tr: Bars during the trade's open window
view.vwap_slippage.h2.typical_price_roll: Typical price + rolling VWAP + fill reference
view.vwap_slippage.btn.load_demo_200_bar: Load demo (200 bars, fill beats VWAP)
view.vwap_slippage.tip.side: Long = buy entry, want fill below VWAP; Short = sell entry, want fill above
view.vwap_slippage.tip.fill: Your executed fill price for the entry
view.vwap_slippage.tip.bars: 'typical_price volume' per line during the trade's open window; ≥1 bar
view.vwap_slippage.tip.demo: Load 200 demo bars with an intentional below-VWAP long fill
view.vwap_slippage.tip.analyze: Compare fill to VWAP; render slippage stats + chart with fill marker
view.vwap_slippage.hint.cyan_typical_pri:
Cyan = typical price per bar. Yellow = rolling VWAP (the
                benchmark). Magenta dashed = your fill price. For LONG entries you want
                magenta BELOW yellow at trade close; for SHORT entries you want it ABOVE.
view.walk_forward.h2.is_oos_chart: IS vs OOS return per window
view.walk_forward.h2.sharpe_chart: IS vs OOS Sharpe per window
view.walk_forward.h2.baseline_single_bes: Baseline (single best fit, entire series) — for comparison
view.walk_forward.hint.pick_a_long_histo: Pick a long-history symbol (SPY, QQQ, AAPL) and run.
view.walk_forward.hint.if_baseline_total: If baseline ≫ total_OOS, the headline strategy is over-fitted; the parameter set that won on the full history would not have survived rolling re-fits.
view.watchlists.hint.no_symbols_yet_add_: No symbols yet. Add one above.
view.webhooks.empty_kind_chart: No webhooks to chart by kind.
view.webhooks.th.name: Name
view.webull.hint.all_endpoints_used_are_: All endpoints used are read-only: positions, today's orders, account summary. Order entry is intentionally not implemented.
view.yield_curve_pca.hint.each_line_is_o:
Each line is one principal component's loading across tenors. PC1 should be
                approximately flat (level shift), PC2 monotone (slope), PC3 bowed (curvature).
view.yield_curve_pca.hint.empirically_th: Empirically the first 3 factors carry >95% of curve-change variance.
view.dividend_calendar.err.no_watchlists: No watchlists found. Add one under the Watchlists view first.
view.murrey_math.err.backend_returned_nu: Backend returned null — likely degenerate price range
view.pattern_discovery.err.top_k_must_be: top-K must be an integer in [1, 20]
